1	//! `push` subcommand — collapse the dual-repo commit+push+finalize
2	//! ceremony into a single resumable command.
3	//!
4	//! See `notes/chores/chores-05.md > Add push subcommand (0.37.0)` for the
5	//! full design.
6	//!
7	//! Dev-step ladder (expanded from original 4 to 6 after adding an
8	//! integration-test step ahead of the first dogfood):
9	//!
10	//! - `0.37.0-0` — scaffolding: flag surface, `Stage` enum, stub `push()`
11	//! - `0.37.0-1` — state file + stage-dispatch loop with stage stubs;
12	//!   `--status`, `--restart`, `--from`
13	//! - `0.37.0-2` — real stage bodies (commits, bookmarks, push,
14	//!   finalize) + `jj op` snapshot rollback
15	//! - `0.37.0-3` — integration tests + workspace-root refactor
16	//!   (thread `root: &Path` through every stage so fixtures can
17	//!   point them at tempdirs); first `vc-x1 push` dogfood ships
18	//!   this commit
19	//! - `0.37.0-4` — interactivity: two approval gates, `$EDITOR`,
20	//!   message persistence across resumes
21	//! - `0.37.0-5` — polish: `--dry-run`, `--step`, non-tty handling,
22	//!   `.gitignore` coherence warning
23	//! - `0.37.0` — docs + workflow migration (done marker)
24	
25	use std::fs;
26	use std::io::IsTerminal;
27	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
28	
29	use chrono::Utc;
30	use clap::{Args, ValueEnum};
31	use log::{debug, info, warn};
32	
33	use crate::common::{prompt, run};
34	use crate::context::Context;
35	use crate::subcommand::SubcommandRunner;
36	use crate::sync::{current_op_id, op_restore};
37	use crate::toml_simple::toml_load;
38	
39	/// Bookmark the session (`.claude`) repo always advances and
40	/// pushes. The session repo is a linear journal on `main` by
41	/// design — `<bookmark>` names a code-repo bookmark only.
42	const SESSION_BOOKMARK: &str = "main";
43	
44	/// Named stages of the `push` state machine.
45	///
46	/// Used by `--from <stage>` to resume at a specific point and by
47	/// `--status` to report the current position. Ordered top-down so
48	/// `Stage as u8` comparisons reflect progress through the flow.
49	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, ValueEnum)]
50	#[value(rename_all = "kebab-case")]
51	pub enum Stage {
52	    /// Run fmt / clippy / test / install / retest.
53	    Preflight,
54	    /// Present diff for the first approval gate.
55	    Review,
56	    /// Compose / edit the commit message; present for second gate.
57	    Message,
58	    /// Commit the app repo.
59	    CommitApp,
60	    /// Commit the `.claude` session repo (skipped if empty).
61	    CommitClaude,
62	    /// Advance each repo's bookmark to its `@-`: app → `<bookmark>`,
63	    /// session → `main`.
64	    BookmarkSet,
65	    /// `jj git push --bookmark <b> -R .`.
66	    PushApp,
67	    /// `vc-x1 finalize --repo .claude --squash --push main ...`.
68	    FinalizeClaude,
69	}
70	
71	impl Stage {
72	    /// Return the stage's kebab-case string identifier (matches what
73	    /// the CLI accepts via `--from <stage>` and what `PushState`
74	    /// persists to disk).
75	    pub fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {
76	        match self {
77	            Stage::Preflight => "preflight",
78	            Stage::Review => "review",
79	            Stage::Message => "message",
80	            Stage::CommitApp => "commit-app",
81	            Stage::CommitClaude => "commit-claude",
82	            Stage::BookmarkSet => "bookmark-set",
83	            Stage::PushApp => "push-app",
84	            Stage::FinalizeClaude => "finalize-claude",
85	        }
86	    }
87	
88	    /// Parse a kebab-case stage name back into a `Stage`.
89	    ///
90	    /// Unknown names return `None`; callers should surface a helpful
91	    /// error rather than silently substituting a default.
92	    pub fn from_str(name: &str) -> Option<Self> {
93	        match name {
94	            "preflight" => Some(Stage::Preflight),
95	            "review" => Some(Stage::Review),
96	            "message" => Some(Stage::Message),
97	            "commit-app" => Some(Stage::CommitApp),
98	            "commit-claude" => Some(Stage::CommitClaude),
99	            "bookmark-set" => Some(Stage::BookmarkSet),
100	            "push-app" => Some(Stage::PushApp),
101	            "finalize-claude" => Some(Stage::FinalizeClaude),
102	            _ => None,
103	        }
104	    }
105	
106	    /// The stage that follows this one, or `None` when this is the
107	    /// last stage (`FinalizeClaude`).
108	    pub fn next(self) -> Option<Self> {
109	        match self {
110	            Stage::Preflight => Some(Stage::Review),
111	            Stage::Review => Some(Stage::Message),
112	            Stage::Message => Some(Stage::CommitApp),
113	            Stage::CommitApp => Some(Stage::CommitClaude),
114	            Stage::CommitClaude => Some(Stage::BookmarkSet),
115	            Stage::BookmarkSet => Some(Stage::PushApp),
116	            Stage::PushApp => Some(Stage::FinalizeClaude),
117	            Stage::FinalizeClaude => None,
118	        }
119	    }
120	
121	    /// The first stage in the flow — used when no saved state exists.
122	    pub fn first() -> Self {
123	        Stage::Preflight
124	    }
125	}
126	
127	/// CLI arguments for the `push` subcommand.
128	///
129	/// Flag set mirrors the design in `notes/chores/chores-05.md`. Flags are
130	/// parsed in 0.37.0-0; their real effects land across the remaining
131	/// 0.37.0-N steps. See the module docstring for which flags activate
132	/// when.
133	#[derive(Args, Debug)]
134	pub struct PushArgs {
135	    /// Bookmark to advance in the code repo (positional form of
136	    /// `--bookmark`). The session repo always advances `main`.
137	    ///
138	    /// Accepting a positional lets the common case read as `vc-x1 push main`
139	    /// without the `--bookmark` ceremony; `--bookmark` is kept as an
140	    /// alias for scripts and for composition with other args. The two
141	    /// forms conflict if both supplied.
142	    #[arg(value_name = "BOOKMARK", conflicts_with = "bookmark")]
143	    pub bookmark_pos: Option<String>,
144	
145	    /// Bookmark to advance in the code repo (flag form; see positional).
146	    #[arg(long, conflicts_with = "bookmark_pos")]
147	    pub bookmark: Option<String>,
148	
149	    /// Clear any saved state file and start from stage 1.
150	    #[arg(long)]
151	    pub restart: bool,
152	
153	    /// Explicit stage to jump to (advanced / debug use).
154	    #[arg(long, value_name = "STAGE")]
155	    pub from: Option<Stage>,
156	
157	    /// Pause between every stage for an interactive approval gate.
158	    #[arg(long)]
159	    pub step: bool,
160	
161	    /// Print where the saved state thinks we are and exit.
162	    #[arg(long)]
163	    pub status: bool,
164	
165	    /// Re-run `preflight` even on resume (default: skip if last run succeeded).
166	    #[arg(long)]
167	    pub recheck: bool,
168	
169	    /// Stop before `finalize-claude` so it can be run manually.
170	    #[arg(long)]
171	    pub no_finalize: bool,
172	
173	    /// Print the exact commands for every stage without side effects.
174	    #[arg(long)]
175	    pub dry_run: bool,
176	
177	    /// Commit title (skip `$EDITOR` for the message stage).
178	    #[arg(long, value_name = "STR")]
179	    pub title: Option<String>,
180	
181	    /// Commit body (skip `$EDITOR` for the message stage).
182	    #[arg(long, value_name = "STR")]
183	    pub body: Option<String>,
184	
185	    /// Auto-approve interactive prompts (review gate, message-edit
186	    /// confirmation). Required when `--title` / `--body` aren't both
187	    /// supplied in non-interactive contexts.
188	    #[arg(short = 'y', long)]
189	    pub yes: bool,
190	}
191	
192	/// Inputs to the push op, flat, owned, clap-free.
193	///
194	/// Mirrors `PushArgs`, with the two clap bookmark spellings
195	/// (positional `BOOKMARK` and `--bookmark`) collapsed into one
196	/// `bookmark` field. The rest map straight over: `restart`, `from`
197	/// (a `Stage`, a domain type — kept), `step`, `status`, `recheck`,
198	/// `no_finalize`, `dry_run`, `title`, `body`, `yes`.
199	pub struct PushParams {
200	    pub bookmark: Option<String>,
201	    pub restart: bool,
202	    pub from: Option<Stage>,
203	    pub step: bool,
204	    pub status: bool,
205	    /// `--recheck` is parsed by `PushArgs` but not yet wired into the
206	    /// stage machine; carried here so the conversion stays total and
207	    /// the field is in place when the behavior lands.
208	    #[allow(dead_code)]
209	    pub recheck: bool,
210	    pub no_finalize: bool,
211	    pub dry_run: bool,
212	    pub title: Option<String>,
213	    pub body: Option<String>,
214	    pub yes: bool,
215	}
216	
217	impl From<&PushArgs> for PushParams {
218	    /// Convert clap-derived `PushArgs` into the flat `PushParams`,
219	    /// collapsing `bookmark_pos` / `bookmark` (clap already enforces
220	    /// they don't both appear) into the single `bookmark` field.
221	    fn from(a: &PushArgs) -> Self {
222	        Self {
223	            bookmark: a.bookmark_pos.clone().or_else(|| a.bookmark.clone()),
224	            restart: a.restart,
225	            from: a.from,
226	            step: a.step,
227	            status: a.status,
228	            recheck: a.recheck,
229	            no_finalize: a.no_finalize,
230	            dry_run: a.dry_run,
231	            title: a.title.clone(),
232	            body: a.body.clone(),
233	            yes: a.yes,
234	        }
235	    }
236	}
237	
238	impl SubcommandRunner for PushArgs {
239	    type Params = PushParams;
240	
241	    /// Delegate to the existing `From<&PushArgs>` impl above
242	    /// (total — never fails).
243	    fn to_params(&self) -> Result<Self::Params, String> {
244	        Ok(PushParams::from(self))
245	    }
246	
247	    /// Run the existing `push` op.
248	    fn run(ctx: &Context, params: &Self::Params) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
249	        push(ctx, params)
250	    }
251	}
252	
253	/// Current state-file format version — bump when the flat key set
254	/// changes incompatibly so readers can detect stale state and refuse
255	/// to resume instead of silently misinterpreting fields.
256	const STATE_FORMAT_VERSION: u32 = 1;
257	
258	/// Default directory (relative to app-repo root) for the push state
259	/// file when `.vc-config.toml` has no `[push]` override.
260	const DEFAULT_STATE_DIR: &str = ".vc-x1";
261	
262	/// Default filename for the push state file under `state_dir`.
263	const DEFAULT_STATE_FILE: &str = "push-state.toml";
264	
265	/// Resolved state-file layout — just the full path today, structured
266	/// so future additions (e.g. a `dir` exposed for `.gitignore`
267	/// coherence checks in 0.37.0-3) have a place to land without
268	/// changing callers.
269	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
270	pub struct StateLayout {
271	    /// Full path `<repo>/<state-dir>/<state-file>`.
272	    pub path: PathBuf,
273	}
274	
275	/// Read `state_dir` / `state_file` from `<repo_root>/.vc-config.toml`
276	/// under the `[push]` section, falling back to defaults (`.vc-x1`
277	/// and `push-state.toml`).
278	///
279	/// Missing config file is not an error — every workspace has the
280	/// defaults applied silently so `vc-x1 push --status` works on a
281	/// fresh clone without ceremony.
282	pub fn resolve_state_layout(repo_root: &Path) -> StateLayout {
283	    let config_path = repo_root.join(".vc-config.toml");
284	    let (dir, file) = if config_path.exists() {
285	        match toml_load(&config_path) {
286	            Ok(map) => {
287	                let dir = map
288	                    .get("push.state-dir")
289	                    .cloned()
290	                    .unwrap_or_else(|| DEFAULT_STATE_DIR.to_string());
291	                let file = map
292	                    .get("push.state-file")
293	                    .cloned()
294	                    .unwrap_or_else(|| DEFAULT_STATE_FILE.to_string());
295	                (dir, file)
296	            }
297	            Err(e) => {
298	                debug!("push: ignoring unparseable .vc-config.toml: {e}");
299	                (
300	                    DEFAULT_STATE_DIR.to_string(),
301	                    DEFAULT_STATE_FILE.to_string(),
302	                )
303	            }
304	        }
305	    } else {
306	        (
307	            DEFAULT_STATE_DIR.to_string(),
308	            DEFAULT_STATE_FILE.to_string(),
309	        )
310	    };
311	    let path = repo_root.join(&dir).join(&file);
312	    StateLayout { path }
313	}
314	
315	/// Persistent state for an in-progress `push` run.
316	///
317	/// Serialized as flat TOML (`key = "value"`) under the dir/file
318	/// configured in `.vc-config.toml`'s `[push]` section. Fields added
319	/// across dev steps are all `Option<_>` so older states remain
320	/// loadable — only `version` / `stage` / `bookmark` / `started_at`
321	/// are required. See the module docstring for which dev step
322	/// introduced which field.
323	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
324	pub struct PushState {
325	    /// State-file format version; must match `STATE_FORMAT_VERSION`
326	    /// to be considered readable.
327	    pub version: u32,
328	    /// The next stage to execute on resume.
329	    pub stage: Stage,
330	    /// Code-repo bookmark being advanced by this run (persisted so
331	    /// resume doesn't need `--bookmark` again). The session repo's
332	    /// side is pinned to `SESSION_BOOKMARK`, not stored here.
333	    pub bookmark: String,
334	    /// ISO-8601 UTC timestamp captured when the state was first
335	    /// written. Informational — helps the user spot stale state.
336	    pub started_at: String,
337	    /// App-repo changeID captured at `message` stage (before
338	    /// `commit-app` runs). Stable across `jj commit` — becomes the
339	    /// chid of the just-committed change. Used when composing the
340	    /// `.claude` commit's ochid trailer. Added in 0.37.0-2.
341	    pub app_chid: Option<String>,
342	    /// `.claude` repo changeID used by the app-repo commit's ochid
343	    /// trailer. Either the pre-commit `@` chid (when `.claude` has
344	    /// pending changes — becomes `@-` after commit) or the current
345	    /// `@-` chid (when `.claude` is clean — stays stable). Added in
346	    /// 0.37.0-2.
347	    pub claude_chid: Option<String>,
348	    /// Whether `.claude`'s working copy had changes at `message`
349	    /// time. Decides whether `commit-claude` actually runs or
350	    /// skips. Added in 0.37.0-2.
351	    pub claude_had_changes: Option<bool>,
352	    /// `jj op` id of the app repo captured before `commit-app`. On
353	    /// failure in stages 4-6, `jj op restore` rewinds here. Added
354	    /// in 0.37.0-2.
355	    pub op_app: Option<String>,
356	    /// `jj op` id of `.claude` captured before `commit-app`. Same
357	    /// rollback target as `op_app`. Added in 0.37.0-2.
358	    pub op_claude: Option<String>,
359	    /// Composed commit title — persisted so resume doesn't need
360	    /// `--title` re-passed. Set during `message` stage from either
361	    /// `--title` or `$EDITOR`. Added in 0.37.0-4.
362	    pub title: Option<String>,
363	    /// Composed commit body (sans ochid trailer, which each commit
364	    /// stage appends). Persisted alongside title. Multi-line
365	    /// content is escaped for the flat-TOML save format (see
366	    /// `escape_multiline` / `unescape_multiline`). Added in
367	    /// 0.37.0-4.
368	    pub body: Option<String>,
369	}
370	
371	impl PushState {
372	    /// Build a fresh state for a new run.
373	    pub fn new_for(bookmark: &str) -> Self {
374	        PushState {
375	            version: STATE_FORMAT_VERSION,
376	            stage: Stage::first(),
377	            bookmark: bookmark.to_string(),
378	            started_at: Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),
379	            app_chid: None,
380	            claude_chid: None,
381	            claude_had_changes: None,
382	            op_app: None,
383	            op_claude: None,
384	            title: None,
385	            body: None,
386	        }
387	    }
388	
389	    /// Write the state to `path`, creating parent dirs as needed.
390	    ///
391	    /// Values are single-line and contain no `"` characters under
392	    /// normal operation (stage names are kebab-case, chids are
393	    /// ASCII, bookmark names don't contain quotes, timestamps are
394	    /// ASCII). A defensive escape pass replaces any stray `"` with
395	    /// `\"` so the file always parses with `toml_simple`. Optional
396	    /// fields are only emitted when set so older state files don't
397	    /// carry a wall of blank keys.
398	    pub fn save(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
399	        if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {
400	            fs::create_dir_all(parent)?;
401	        }
402	        let mut content = String::new();
403	        content.push_str("# vc-x1 push state — managed file, do not edit by hand\n");
404	        content.push_str("[push-state]\n");
405	        content.push_str(&format!("version = {}\n", self.version));
406	        content.push_str(&format!("stage = \"{}\"\n", self.stage.as_str()));
407	        content.push_str(&format!("bookmark = \"{}\"\n", escape_toml(&self.bookmark)));
408	        content.push_str(&format!(
409	            "started_at = \"{}\"\n",
410	            escape_toml(&self.started_at)
411	        ));
412	        if let Some(v) = &self.app_chid {
413	            content.push_str(&format!("app_chid = \"{}\"\n", escape_toml(v)));
414	        }
415	        if let Some(v) = &self.claude_chid {
416	            content.push_str(&format!("claude_chid = \"{}\"\n", escape_toml(v)));
417	        }
418	        if let Some(v) = self.claude_had_changes {
419	            content.push_str(&format!("claude_had_changes = {v}\n"));
420	        }
421	        if let Some(v) = &self.op_app {
422	            content.push_str(&format!("op_app = \"{}\"\n", escape_toml(v)));
423	        }
424	        if let Some(v) = &self.op_claude {
425	            content.push_str(&format!("op_claude = \"{}\"\n", escape_toml(v)));
426	        }
427	        if let Some(v) = &self.title {
428	            content.push_str(&format!("title = \"{}\"\n", escape_multiline(v)));
429	        }
430	        if let Some(v) = &self.body {
431	            content.push_str(&format!("body = \"{}\"\n", escape_multiline(v)));
432	        }
433	        fs::write(path, content)?;
434	        debug!("push: wrote state to {}", path.display());
435	        Ok(())
436	    }
437	
438	    /// Load state from `path`, returning `Ok(None)` if the file is
439	    /// absent (a fresh run) and `Err` if the file exists but is
440	    /// unusable (stale format, missing required keys, unknown stage).
441	    pub fn load(path: &Path) -> Result<Option<Self>, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
442	        if !path.exists() {
443	            return Ok(None);
444	        }
445	        let map = toml_load(path)?;
446	        let require = |k: &str| -> Result<String, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
447	            map.get(k)
448	                .cloned()
449	                .ok_or_else(|| format!("push state {}: missing key '{k}'", path.display()).into())
450	        };
451	        let version: u32 = require("push-state.version")?
452	            .parse()
453	            .map_err(|e| format!("push state {}: invalid version: {e}", path.display()))?;
454	        if version != STATE_FORMAT_VERSION {
455	            return Err(format!(
456	                "push state {}: unsupported format version {version} (expected {}); \
457	                 re-run with --restart to start fresh",
458	                path.display(),
459	                STATE_FORMAT_VERSION
460	            )
461	            .into());
462	        }
463	        let stage_str = require("push-state.stage")?;
464	        let stage = Stage::from_str(&stage_str)
465	            .ok_or_else(|| format!("push state {}: unknown stage '{stage_str}'", path.display()))?;
466	        let claude_had_changes = match map.get("push-state.claude_had_changes") {
467	            Some(s) => Some(s.parse::<bool>().map_err(|e| {
468	                format!(
469	                    "push state {}: invalid claude_had_changes: {e}",
470	                    path.display()
471	                )
472	            })?),
473	            None => None,
474	        };
475	        Ok(Some(PushState {
476	            version,
477	            stage,
478	            bookmark: require("push-state.bookmark")?,
479	            started_at: require("push-state.started_at")?,
480	            app_chid: map.get("push-state.app_chid").cloned(),
481	            claude_chid: map.get("push-state.claude_chid").cloned(),
482	            claude_had_changes,
483	            op_app: map.get("push-state.op_app").cloned(),
484	            op_claude: map.get("push-state.op_claude").cloned(),
485	            title: map.get("push-state.title").map(|s| unescape_multiline(s)),
486	            body: map.get("push-state.body").map(|s| unescape_multiline(s)),
487	        }))
488	    }
489	}
490	
491	/// Escape any `"` in a value so the single-line TOML strings we emit
492	/// stay parseable. `toml_simple` trims the surrounding quotes on read
493	/// but doesn't process escapes, so we just avoid characters that
494	/// would break the single-line form.
495	fn escape_toml(s: &str) -> String {
496	    s.replace('"', "\\\"")
497	}
498	
499	/// Escape a potentially multi-line string for persistence in a
500	/// single-line TOML value. `toml_simple` only handles one line per
501	/// value so we encode newlines as `\n`, tabs as `\t`, and any stray
502	/// `"` / `\` as their escaped forms. The inverse is
503	/// `unescape_multiline`.
504	fn escape_multiline(s: &str) -> String {
505	    let mut out = String::with_capacity(s.len());
506	    for ch in s.chars() {
507	        match ch {
508	            '\\' => out.push_str("\\\\"),
509	            '"' => out.push_str("\\\""),
510	            '\n' => out.push_str("\\n"),
511	            '\t' => out.push_str("\\t"),
512	            '\r' => out.push_str("\\r"),
513	            c => out.push(c),
514	        }
515	    }
516	    out
517	}
518	
519	/// Invert `escape_multiline`. Unknown backslash-escapes pass through
520	/// untouched (best-effort — this is a managed state file, so it's
521	/// unlikely to encounter hand-edited escapes).
522	fn unescape_multiline(s: &str) -> String {
523	    let mut out = String::with_capacity(s.len());
524	    let mut chars = s.chars();
525	    while let Some(ch) = chars.next() {
526	        if ch == '\\' {
527	            match chars.next() {
528	                Some('\\') => out.push('\\'),
529	                Some('"') => out.push('"'),
530	                Some('n') => out.push('\n'),
531	                Some('t') => out.push('\t'),
532	                Some('r') => out.push('\r'),
533	                Some(other) => {
534	                    out.push('\\');
535	                    out.push(other);
536	                }
537	                None => out.push('\\'),
538	            }
539	        } else {
540	            out.push(ch);
541	        }
542	    }
543	    out
544	}
545	
546	/// Whether `stdin` is attached to a terminal.
547	///
548	/// Interactive prompts (`stage_review`, `$EDITOR` launch in
549	/// `stage_message`, `--step` gates) need this to fail fast in
550	/// scripted / CI contexts instead of hanging on `read_line`.
551	/// `--yes` overrides the check — script callers opt in by
552	/// asserting "all prompts auto-approved".
553	fn is_stdin_tty() -> bool {
554	    std::io::stdin().is_terminal()
555	}
556	
557	/// Verify the configured state dir is matched by a `.gitignore` entry,
558	/// erroring if not. The check is intentionally simple — looks for
559	/// `<state_dir>` or `/<state_dir>` as a line in `.gitignore` at the
560	/// repo root. Misses trickier patterns (nested wildcards, ignore files
561	/// further down the tree) but catches the common "user changed the
562	/// config and forgot to update .gitignore" case, which is the whole
563	/// point. Fatal as of 0.37.1 — the warning was easy to miss in the
564	/// preflight wall of output, and a committed state file is a real
565	/// foot-gun. `vc-x1 init` and `vc-x1 test-fixture` write `/.vc-x1`
566	/// into their generated `.gitignore` so the fresh-repo path is clean.
567	fn check_gitignore_coherence(
568	    root: &Path,
569	    state_dir_name: &str,
570	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
571	    let gitignore = root.join(".gitignore");
572	    let matched = match fs::read_to_string(&gitignore) {
573	        Ok(content) => content.lines().any(|line| {
574	            let l = line.trim();
575	            l == state_dir_name
576	                || l == format!("/{state_dir_name}")
577	                || l == format!("{state_dir_name}/")
578	                || l == format!("/{state_dir_name}/")
579	        }),
580	        Err(_) => false,
581	    };
582	    if !matched {
583	        return Err(format!(
584	            "push: state dir '{state_dir_name}' is not in {} — \
585	             add a '/{state_dir_name}' line so in-progress push-state \
586	             files don't get committed",
587	            gitignore.display()
588	        )
589	        .into());
590	    }
591	    Ok(())
592	}
593	
594	/// Entry point for the `push` subcommand.
595	///
596	/// 0.37.0-5 behavior: interactive flow with two approval gates
597	/// (review + message) plus polish flags: `--dry-run` prints what
598	/// would run without side effects, `--step` pauses between every
599	/// stage, non-tty stdin fails fast when prompts are required,
600	/// `--yes` opts out of all prompts. On any failure in stages 4-6
601	/// (the local mutation window between `commit-app` and
602	/// `bookmark-set`), both repos roll back to the `jj op` snapshot
603	/// recorded at the start of `commit-app`. After `push-app` the
604	/// remote boundary is crossed and recovery is forward-only.
605	///
606	/// `ctx` is unused today (push reads `.vc-config.toml` for its
607	/// state-file layout and hard-codes the finalize `--log` path); it's
608	/// present for the uniform subcommand-layer signature.
609	pub fn push(_ctx: &Context, params: &PushParams) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
610	    let cwd = std::env::current_dir()?;
611	    push_in(&cwd, params)
612	}
613	
614	/// `push` parameterized on the workspace root. CLI dispatch calls
615	/// this with `std::env::current_dir()`; integration tests call it
616	/// with a fixture tempdir so the stage bodies mutate the fixture's
617	/// repos instead of the developer's working tree.
618	pub(crate) fn push_in(
619	    workspace_root: &Path,
620	    params: &PushParams,
621	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
622	    let layout = resolve_state_layout(workspace_root);
623	
624	    if params.status {
625	        return cmd_status(&layout);
626	    }
627	
628	    if params.dry_run {
629	        info!("push: DRY-RUN — no side effects (no commits, no pushes, no state written)");
630	    }
631	
632	    // Gitignore coherence check (fatal). Only checked when we
633	    // actually have a file path with a parent (resolve_state_layout
634	    // always gives one, so this is effectively always-on).
635	    if let Some(dir_name) = layout
636	        .path
637	        .parent()
638	        .and_then(|p| p.file_name())
639	        .and_then(|s| s.to_str())
640	    {
641	        check_gitignore_coherence(workspace_root, dir_name)?;
642	    }
643	
644	    if params.restart && layout.path.exists() {
645	        fs::remove_file(&layout.path)?;
646	        debug!("push: --restart cleared state at {}", layout.path.display());
647	    }
648	
649	    // Load existing state, or require a bookmark (positional or flag)
650	    // to bootstrap one.
651	    let mut state = match PushState::load(&layout.path)? {
652	        Some(s) => s,
653	        None => {
654	            let bookmark = params.bookmark.as_deref().ok_or(
655	                "push: no saved state; a bookmark is required to start a new run \
656	                 — pass it as a positional (`vc-x1 push main`) or via `--bookmark main`",
657	            )?;
658	            PushState::new_for(bookmark)
659	        }
660	    };
661	
662	    // `--from` overrides the resumed stage (does not affect bookmark
663	    // or other persisted fields).
664	    if let Some(from) = params.from {
665	        state.stage = from;
666	    }
667	
668	    run_from(workspace_root, &mut state, params, &layout)
669	}
670	
671	/// Full path of the `.claude` session repo for a given workspace
672	/// root. Centralized so a future layout change (e.g. configurable
673	/// session-repo name) has one caller to update.
674	fn claude_path(workspace_root: &Path) -> PathBuf {
675	    workspace_root.join(".claude")
676	}
677	
678	/// Print the resumed stage (or "no saved state") and return.
679	fn cmd_status(layout: &StateLayout) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
680	    match PushState::load(&layout.path)? {
681	        Some(state) => {
682	            info!(
683	                "push-state: stage={} bookmark={} started={} (file: {})",
684	                state.stage.as_str(),
685	                state.bookmark,
686	                state.started_at,
687	                layout.path.display()
688	            );
689	        }
690	        None => {
691	            info!(
692	                "push-state: no saved state ({} does not exist)",
693	                layout.path.display()
694	            );
695	        }
696	    }
697	    Ok(())
698	}
699	
700	/// Walk the state machine from `state.stage` to the end, saving
701	/// progress after each stage.
702	///
703	/// Records a `jj op` snapshot in both repos the first time we enter
704	/// `commit-app` and leaves it in state so resume inherits the
705	/// rollback target. On failure inside the rollback-eligible window
706	/// (`commit-app` / `commit-claude` / `bookmark-set`), both repos
707	/// are restored before the error propagates.
708	fn run_from(
709	    root: &Path,
710	    state: &mut PushState,
711	    params: &PushParams,
712	    layout: &StateLayout,
713	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
714	    // State-sanity preflight: catch stale-state-after-out-of-band-recovery
715	    // (no-op on fresh state). Runs before save so a bogus halt point isn't
716	    // re-persisted. Per chores-06 [61] design.
717	    verify_state_sanity(root, state)?;
718	
719	    // Only persist state in real runs. Dry-runs are inspection-only
720	    // — carrying their inferred chids / op snapshots into real runs
721	    // would mislead the user about what was actually recorded.
722	    if !params.dry_run {
723	        state.save(&layout.path)?;
724	    }
725	    loop {
726	        let stage = state.stage;
727	
728	        // Snapshot op ids once on first `commit-app` entry. Skipped
729	        // in dry-run since we won't mutate anything to roll back.
730	        if stage == Stage::CommitApp && state.op_app.is_none() && !params.dry_run {
731	            state.op_app = Some(current_op_id(root)?);
732	            state.op_claude = Some(current_op_id(&claude_path(root))?);
733	            state.save(&layout.path)?;
734	        }
735	
736	        let result = run_stage(root, stage, state, params, layout);
737	
738	        if let Err(e) = &result {
739	            if stage_is_rollback_eligible(stage) && !params.dry_run {
740	                rollback_on_failure(root, state, e.as_ref());
741	            }
742	            return result;
743	        }
744	
745	        let next = stage.next();
746	
747	        // --step: pause between every stage so the user can inspect
748	        // intermediate state before the next one runs. Only if
749	        // there's a next stage to gate, and skipped entirely when
750	        // --yes is set or stdin isn't a tty (the prompt would hang
751	        // in scripted contexts — the script is presumed consenting).
752	        if params.step && next.is_some() {
753	            if params.yes {
754	                debug!(
755	                    "push step: --yes skips step gate between {} and next",
756	                    stage.as_str()
757	                );
758	            } else if !is_stdin_tty() {
759	                return Err("push: --step requires a tty (stdin is not interactive); \
760	                            add --yes to bypass step gates in non-interactive contexts"
761	                    .into());
762	            } else {
763	                let answer = prompt(&format!(
764	                    "push step: {} done. Continue to {}? [y/N] ",
765	                    stage.as_str(),
766	                    next.map(Stage::as_str).unwrap_or("")
767	                ))?;
768	                let normalized = answer.trim().to_ascii_lowercase();
769	                if normalized != "y" && normalized != "yes" {
770	                    return Err(format!(
771	                        "push: step gate declined after {} (got {answer:?})",
772	                        stage.as_str()
773	                    )
774	                    .into());
775	                }
776	            }
777	        }
778	
779	        match next {
780	            Some(n) => {
781	                state.stage = n;
782	                if !params.dry_run {
783	                    state.save(&layout.path)?;
784	                }
785	            }
786	            None => break,
787	        }
788	    }
789	    if params.dry_run {
790	        info!("push: DRY-RUN complete — no changes written");
791	    } else {
792	        // Honest completion: verify the world matches state before
793	        // declaring success. Catches the 0.37.1 false-success symptom
794	        // (stages "ran" but didn't actually commit / push what state
795	        // says they did). Warning-only because work has already
796	        // crossed the remote boundary by this point — rollback isn't
797	        // sound, the user needs to investigate.
798	        match verify_completion_sanity(root, state) {
799	            Ok(()) => {
800	                if layout.path.exists() {
801	                    fs::remove_file(&layout.path)?;
802	                }
803	                info!("push: completed all stages (verified, state cleared)");
804	            }
805	            Err(e) => {
806	                if layout.path.exists() {
807	                    fs::remove_file(&layout.path)?;
808	                }
809	                warn!("push: completed stages but post-completion verification failed:");
810	                warn!("  {e}");
811	                warn!(
812	                    "  state cleared anyway (work landed on remote); investigate the \
813	                     discrepancy before next push"
814	                );
815	            }
816	        }
817	    }
818	    Ok(())
819	}
820	
821	/// Whether a failure in `stage` should trigger the cross-repo op
822	/// restore. Anything at or past `push-app` crosses the remote
823	/// boundary — the app commit is live on origin, rollback is no
824	/// longer sound — so those failures propagate without touching
825	/// snapshots.
826	fn stage_is_rollback_eligible(stage: Stage) -> bool {
827	    matches!(
828	        stage,
829	        Stage::CommitApp | Stage::CommitClaude | Stage::BookmarkSet
830	    )
831	}
832	
833	/// Restore both repos to their `jj op` snapshots, if we have them.
834	///
835	/// Best-effort — if the restore itself fails we warn but don't
836	/// shadow the original error the caller will propagate. Exposed at
837	/// `pub(crate)` so integration tests can exercise the rollback path
838	/// directly rather than forcing a stage failure.
839	pub(crate) fn rollback_on_failure(
840	    root: &Path,
841	    state: &PushState,
842	    original: &dyn std::error::Error,
843	) {
844	    warn!("push: rolling back both repos after: {original}");
845	    if let Some(op) = &state.op_app {
846	        match op_restore(root, op) {
847	            Ok(()) => info!("push: restored app repo to op {op}"),
848	            Err(e) => warn!("push: app repo restore failed: {e}"),
849	        }
850	    }
851	    if let Some(op) = &state.op_claude {
852	        match op_restore(&claude_path(root), op) {
853	            Ok(()) => info!("push: restored .claude to op {op}"),
854	            Err(e) => warn!("push: .claude restore failed: {e}"),
855	        }
856	    }
857	}
858	
859	/// Execute one stage. Arms mirror `Stage`'s declaration order.
860	fn run_stage(
861	    root: &Path,
862	    stage: Stage,
863	    state: &mut PushState,
864	    params: &PushParams,
865	    layout: &StateLayout,
866	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
867	    match stage {
868	        Stage::Preflight => stage_preflight(root, state, params),
869	        Stage::Review => stage_review(root, params),
870	        Stage::Message => stage_message(root, state, params, layout),
871	        Stage::CommitApp => stage_commit_app(root, state, params),
872	        Stage::CommitClaude => stage_commit_claude(root, state, params),
873	        Stage::BookmarkSet => stage_bookmark_set(root, state, params),
874	        Stage::PushApp => stage_push_app(root, state, params),
875	        Stage::FinalizeClaude => stage_finalize_claude(root, state, params),
876	    }
877	}
878	
879	/// Preflight: `vc-x1 sync --check && cargo fmt && cargo clippy
880	/// -D warnings && cargo test`.
881	///
882	/// Sync runs in the hidden deprecated `--check` mode so divergence
883	/// with the remote is surfaced before we burn cargo cycles, but
884	/// **not** auto-resolved — a rebase mid-push is exactly the kind of
885	/// unsupervised mutation the two approval gates exist to prevent. If
886	/// sync reports action needed, preflight errors and the user
887	/// resolves explicitly with `vc-x1 sync` before re-running push
888	/// (rewiring this shell-out in-process is a `notes/todo.md` sync
889	/// follow-up). The cargo steps
890	/// match AGENTS.md's pre-commit checklist (minus `cargo install` /
891	/// retest, which are project-specific). All subprocesses run in the
892	/// workspace root so cargo picks up the right `Cargo.toml`. Skipped
893	/// in `--dry-run` since `cargo fmt` writes files.
894	fn stage_preflight(
895	    root: &Path,
896	    state: &PushState,
897	    params: &PushParams,
898	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
899	    if params.dry_run {
900	        info!(
901	            "push preflight: [dry-run] would run verify-tracking / vc-x1 sync --check / cargo fmt / clippy / test"
902	        );
903	        return Ok(());
904	    }
905	    info!("push preflight: verify bookmark tracking");
906	    crate::common::verify_tracking(root, &state.bookmark)?;
907	    crate::common::verify_tracking(&claude_path(root), SESSION_BOOKMARK)?;
908	    info!("push preflight: vc-x1 sync --check");
909	    run("vc-x1", &["sync", "--check"], root)?;
910	    info!("push preflight: cargo fmt");
911	    run("cargo", &["fmt"], root)?;
912	    info!("push preflight: cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings");
913	    run(
914	        "cargo",
915	        &["clippy", "--all-targets", "--", "-D", "warnings"],
916	        root,
917	    )?;
918	    info!("push preflight: cargo test");
919	    run("cargo", &["test"], root)?;
920	    Ok(())
921	}
922	
923	/// Review: first approval gate — "is the work done right?".
924	///
925	/// Shows a `jj diff --stat` of the pending changes in both repos
926	/// and prompts the user to continue. `--yes` short-circuits the
927	/// prompt (required for scripted / non-tty use). In `--dry-run`
928	/// the diff is still shown (that's the point of dry-run — see
929	/// what *would* be reviewed) but approval is auto-granted.
930	fn stage_review(root: &Path, params: &PushParams) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
931	    let claude = claude_path(root);
932	    let app_arg = root.to_string_lossy();
933	    let claude_arg = claude.to_string_lossy();
934	    info!("push review: pending changes:");
935	    info!("  app ({app_arg}):");
936	    let app_stat = run("jj", &["diff", "--stat", "-R", &app_arg], root)?;
937	    for line in app_stat.lines() {
938	        info!("    {line}");
939	    }
940	    info!("  .claude ({claude_arg}):");
941	    let claude_stat = run("jj", &["diff", "--stat", "-R", &claude_arg], root)?;
942	    for line in claude_stat.lines() {
943	        info!("    {line}");
944	    }
945	    if params.yes {
946	        info!("push review: auto-approved (--yes)");
947	        return Ok(());
948	    }
949	    if params.dry_run {
950	        info!("push review: [dry-run] auto-approved");
951	        return Ok(());
952	    }
953	    if !is_stdin_tty() {
954	        return Err("push review: stdin is not a tty; \
955	                    pass --yes to auto-approve in non-interactive contexts"
956	            .into());
957	    }
958	    let answer = prompt("push review: approve and continue to message stage? [y/N] ")?;
959	    let normalized = answer.trim().to_ascii_lowercase();
960	    if normalized == "y" || normalized == "yes" {
961	        Ok(())
962	    } else {
963	        Err(format!(
964	            "push review: declined (got {answer:?}); re-run with --yes or confirm with 'y'"
965	        )
966	        .into())
967	    }
968	}
969	
970	/// Message: collect pre-commit changeIDs and record whether
971	/// `.claude` has pending changes so `commit-claude` can skip when
972	/// empty. `--title` and `--body` are required in 0.37.0-2; `$EDITOR`
973	/// support + message persistence land in 0.37.0-3.
974	fn stage_message(
975	    root: &Path,
976	    state: &mut PushState,
977	    params: &PushParams,
978	    layout: &StateLayout,
979	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
980	    // Resolve title/body by priority:
981	    //   1. --title / --body flags (both must be present to skip editor)
982	    //   2. persisted title/body from prior stage run (resume case)
983	    //   3. $EDITOR template (interactive); fails if --yes and nothing else
984	    let (title, body) = match (params.title.clone(), params.body.clone()) {
985	        (Some(t), Some(b)) => (t, b),
986	        _ => match (state.title.clone(), state.body.clone()) {
987	            (Some(t), Some(b)) => (t, b),
988	            _ => {
989	                if params.yes {
990	                    return Err("push message: --yes given but --title/--body missing \
991	                                and no persisted message to resume — pass both flags \
992	                                or run interactively."
993	                        .into());
994	                }
995	                if params.dry_run {
996	                    return Err("push message: --dry-run given but --title/--body missing \
997	                                and no persisted message — pass both flags so dry-run \
998	                                has a message to preview."
999	                        .into());
1000	                }
1001	                if !is_stdin_tty() {
1002	                    return Err("push message: stdin is not a tty and no --title/--body \
1003	                                supplied; cannot launch $EDITOR in a non-interactive \
1004	                                context. Pass --title and --body, or run interactively."
1005	                        .into());
1006	                }
1007	                compose_message_via_editor(layout)?
1008	            }
1009	        },
1010	    };
1011	
1012	    // Persist the composed message so a later resume (e.g. after a
1013	    // commit-app retry) doesn't need the flags re-passed.
1014	    state.title = Some(title.clone());
1015	    state.body = Some(body.clone());
1016	
1017	    let claude = claude_path(root);
1018	    let app_chid = get_change_id(root, "@")?;
1019	    let claude_empty = jj_log_empty(&claude, "@")?;
1020	    let claude_had_changes = !claude_empty;
1021	    let claude_ref = if claude_had_changes { "@" } else { "@-" };
1022	    let claude_chid = get_change_id(&claude, claude_ref)?;
1023	
1024	    info!(
1025	        "push message: title=\"{}\", app_chid={app_chid}, claude_chid={claude_chid}, claude_had_changes={claude_had_changes}",
1026	        title.lines().next().unwrap_or("") // OK: obvious
1027	    );
1028	    state.app_chid = Some(app_chid);
1029	    state.claude_chid = Some(claude_chid);
1030	    state.claude_had_changes = Some(claude_had_changes);
1031	    Ok(())
1032	}
1033	
1034	/// Launch `$EDITOR` (falling back to `vi`) on a template file under
1035	/// `state_dir`, then parse the saved content into a `(title, body)`
1036	/// tuple. Lines starting with `#` are treated as comments and
1037	/// stripped. Empty input aborts the push.
1038	fn compose_message_via_editor(
1039	    layout: &StateLayout,
1040	) -> Result<(String, String), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
1041	    let editor = std::env::var("EDITOR")
1042	        .or_else(|_| std::env::var("VISUAL"))
1043	        .unwrap_or_else(|_| "vi".to_string()); // OK: POSIX fallback when nothing is configured
1044	    let msg_path = layout
1045	        .path
1046	        .parent()
1047	        .ok_or("push message: state layout has no parent dir")?
1048	        .join("push-message.txt");
1049	    if let Some(parent) = msg_path.parent() {
1050	        fs::create_dir_all(parent)?;
1051	    }
1052	    let template = "\
1053	# Leave as-is to abort. Enter the Title on the first line,
1054	# optionally followed by the Body on subsequent lines. The
1055	# blank line between Title and Body is inserted automatically,
1056	# and the ochid trailer is appended per-repo automatically —
1057	# don't add either here.
1058	# Lines starting with `#` are ignored.
1059	";
1060	    fs::write(&msg_path, template)?;
1061	    info!("push message: launching {editor} on {}", msg_path.display());
1062	    let status = std::process::Command::new(&editor)
1063	        .arg(&msg_path)
1064	        .status()
1065	        .map_err(|e| format!("failed to launch {editor}: {e}"))?;
1066	    if !status.success() {
1067	        return Err(format!("{editor} exited non-zero ({status})").into());
1068	    }
1069	    let content = fs::read_to_string(&msg_path)?;
1070	    let _ = fs::remove_file(&msg_path);
1071	    parse_message(&content)
1072	        .ok_or_else(|| "push message: template was left empty or all-comments — aborting".into())
1073	}
1074	
1075	/// Parse the editor-saved message into `(title, body)`. Strips
1076	/// `#`-prefixed comment lines, trims surrounding blanks, and treats
1077	/// the first non-comment line as the title with everything after as
1078	/// the body (a blank line between is allowed but not required —
1079	/// the commit stages insert the title/body separator themselves).
1080	/// Returns `None` when the message has no non-comment content.
1081	fn parse_message(raw: &str) -> Option<(String, String)> {
1082	    let mut meaningful = String::new();
1083	    for line in raw.lines() {
1084	        let trimmed_left = line.trim_start();
1085	        if trimmed_left.starts_with('#') {
1086	            continue;
1087	        }
1088	        meaningful.push_str(line);
1089	        meaningful.push('\n');
1090	    }
1091	    let trimmed = meaningful.trim();
1092	    if trimmed.is_empty() {
1093	        return None;
1094	    }
1095	    let mut lines = trimmed.lines();
1096	    let title = lines.next().unwrap_or("").trim().to_string(); // OK: trimmed non-empty ⇒ first line exists
1097	    let body = lines.collect::<Vec<_>>().join("\n").trim().to_string();
1098	    if title.is_empty() {
1099	        return None;
1100	    }
1101	    Some((title, body))
1102	}
1103	
1104	/// Commit app repo with `title` / `body` and the `ochid:` trailer
1105	/// pointing at `.claude`'s chid.
1106	fn stage_commit_app(
1107	    root: &Path,
1108	    state: &PushState,
1109	    params: &PushParams,
1110	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
1111	    let (title, body) = resolve_message(state, params)?;
1112	    let claude_chid = state
1113	        .claude_chid
1114	        .as_deref()
1115	        .ok_or("push commit-app: claude_chid not set (message stage didn't run)")?;
1116	    let body_with_trailer = format!("{body}\n\nochid: /.claude/{claude_chid}");
1117	    let app_arg = root.to_string_lossy();
1118	    if params.dry_run {
1119	        info!(
1120	            "push commit-app: [dry-run] would run jj commit -R {app_arg} -m \"{title}\" -m <body+ochid>"
1121	        );
1122	        return Ok(());
1123	    }
1124	    info!("push commit-app: jj commit -R {app_arg}");
1125	    run(
1126	        "jj",
1127	        &[
1128	            "commit",
1129	            "-R",
1130	            &app_arg,
1131	            "-m",
1132	            &title,
1133	            "-m",
1134	            &body_with_trailer,
1135	        ],
1136	        root,
1137	    )?;
1138	    Ok(())
1139	}
1140	
1141	/// Pull the title/body pair from CLI args or persisted state,
1142	/// preferring CLI args (override-on-resume case). Returns `Err`
1143	/// when neither source has them — which only happens if
1144	/// `stage_message` didn't run or was force-bypassed.
1145	fn resolve_message(
1146	    state: &PushState,
1147	    params: &PushParams,
1148	) -> Result<(String, String), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
1149	    let title = params
1150	        .title
1151	        .clone()
1152	        .or_else(|| state.title.clone())
1153	        .ok_or("push: title missing — message stage didn't run")?;
1154	    let body = params
1155	        .body
1156	        .clone()
1157	        .or_else(|| state.body.clone())
1158	        .ok_or("push: body missing — message stage didn't run")?;
1159	    Ok((title, body))
1160	}
1161	
1162	/// Commit `.claude` with the same title/body and the ochid trailer
1163	/// pointing at the app commit's chid, or skip if `.claude` had no
1164	/// pending changes.
1165	fn stage_commit_claude(
1166	    root: &Path,
1167	    state: &PushState,
1168	    params: &PushParams,
1169	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
1170	    if !state.claude_had_changes.unwrap_or(false) {
1171	        info!("push commit-claude: skip (.claude had no pending changes)");
1172	        return Ok(());
1173	    }
1174	    let (title, body) = resolve_message(state, params)?;
1175	    let app_chid = state
1176	        .app_chid
1177	        .as_deref()
1178	        .ok_or("push commit-claude: app_chid not set (message stage didn't run)")?;
1179	    let body_with_trailer = format!("{body}\n\nochid: /{app_chid}");
1180	    let claude = claude_path(root);
1181	    let claude_arg = claude.to_string_lossy();
1182	    if params.dry_run {
1183	        info!(
1184	            "push commit-claude: [dry-run] would run jj commit -R {claude_arg} -m \"{title}\" -m <body+ochid>"
1185	        );
1186	        return Ok(());
1187	    }
1188	    info!("push commit-claude: jj commit -R {claude_arg}");
1189	    run(
1190	        "jj",
1191	        &[
1192	            "commit",
1193	            "-R",
1194	            &claude_arg,
1195	            "-m",
1196	            &title,
1197	            "-m",
1198	            &body_with_trailer,
1199	        ],
1200	        root,
1201	    )?;
1202	    Ok(())
1203	}
1204	
1205	/// Advance each repo's bookmark to its `@-`: the app repo advances
1206	/// `state.bookmark`, the session repo always advances
1207	/// `SESSION_BOOKMARK` (`main`) — a feature bookmark must never be
1208	/// created in the linear-journal session repo.
1209	fn stage_bookmark_set(
1210	    root: &Path,
1211	    state: &PushState,
1212	    params: &PushParams,
1213	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
1214	    let bk = &state.bookmark;
1215	    let app_arg = root.to_string_lossy();
1216	    let claude = claude_path(root);
1217	    let claude_arg = claude.to_string_lossy();
1218	    if params.dry_run {
1219	        info!(
1220	            "push bookmark-set: [dry-run] would run jj bookmark set {bk} -r @- -R {app_arg} / {SESSION_BOOKMARK} -r @- -R {claude_arg}"
1221	        );
1222	        return Ok(());
1223	    }
1224	    info!(
1225	        "push bookmark-set: jj bookmark set {bk} -r @- -R {app_arg} / {SESSION_BOOKMARK} -r @- -R {claude_arg}"
1226	    );
1227	    run(
1228	        "jj",
1229	        &["bookmark", "set", bk, "-r", "@-", "-R", &app_arg],
1230	        root,
1231	    )?;
1232	    run(
1233	        "jj",
1234	        &[
1235	            "bookmark",
1236	            "set",
1237	            SESSION_BOOKMARK,
1238	            "-r",
1239	            "@-",
1240	            "-R",
1241	            &claude_arg,
1242	        ],
1243	        root,
1244	    )?;
1245	    Ok(())
1246	}
1247	
1248	/// Push the app repo's bookmark to origin.
1249	fn stage_push_app(
1250	    root: &Path,
1251	    state: &PushState,
1252	    params: &PushParams,
1253	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
1254	    let bk = &state.bookmark;
1255	    let app_arg = root.to_string_lossy();
1256	    if params.dry_run {
1257	        info!("push push-app: [dry-run] would run jj git push --bookmark {bk} -R {app_arg}");
1258	        return Ok(());
1259	    }
1260	    info!("push push-app: jj git push --bookmark {bk} -R {app_arg}");
1261	    run(
1262	        "jj",
1263	        &["git", "push", "--bookmark", bk, "-R", &app_arg],
1264	        root,
1265	    )?;
1266	    Ok(())
1267	}
1268	
1269	/// Finalize `.claude` via an out-of-process `vc-x1 finalize` call,
1270	/// always pushing `SESSION_BOOKMARK` (`main`) — the session repo's
1271	/// bookmark is pinned, so `state.bookmark` plays no part here.
1272	/// Shells out rather than calling `finalize::finalize` in-process
1273	/// so `--detach` can fork a child that outlives push's own
1274	/// lifetime. `--no-finalize` turns this stage into a no-op (which
1275	/// is how integration tests avoid spawning a detached process).
1276	fn stage_finalize_claude(
1277	    root: &Path,
1278	    _state: &PushState,
1279	    params: &PushParams,
1280	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
1281	    if params.no_finalize {
1282	        info!("push finalize-claude: skip (--no-finalize)");
1283	        return Ok(());
1284	    }
1285	    let bk = SESSION_BOOKMARK;
1286	    let claude = claude_path(root);
1287	    let claude_arg = claude.to_string_lossy();
1288	    if params.dry_run {
1289	        info!(
1290	            "push finalize-claude: [dry-run] would run vc-x1 finalize --repo {claude_arg} --squash --push {bk} --delay 10 --detach"
1291	        );
1292	        return Ok(());
1293	    }
1294	    info!(
1295	        "push finalize-claude: vc-x1 finalize --repo {claude_arg} --squash --push {bk} --delay 10 --detach"
1296	    );
1297	    run(
1298	        "vc-x1",
1299	        &[
1300	            "finalize",
1301	            "--repo",
1302	            &claude_arg,
1303	            "--squash",
1304	            "--push",
1305	            bk,
1306	            "--delay",
1307	            "10",
1308	            "--detach",
1309	            "--log",
1310	            "/tmp/vc-x1-finalize.log",
1311	        ],
1312	        root,
1313	    )?;
1314	    Ok(())
1315	}
1316	
1317	/// Verify the saved state still matches reality before any stage runs.
1318	///
1319	/// Catches the failure mode where out-of-band activity (manual finalize,
1320	/// squash + force-push, abandon, etc.) moved the world forward between
1321	/// the prior push and this resume, leaving `state` pointing at a
1322	/// now-bogus halt point. The 0.37.1 dogfood incident is the canonical
1323	/// example: push parked at `finalize-claude` after a failure; manual
1324	/// recovery moved the world forward; the next push resumed at the
1325	/// parked stage, no-op'd, and falsely declared "completed all stages"
1326	/// while working copies still held uncommitted changes.
1327	///
1328	/// Three checks (per chores-06 [61] design):
1329	///
1330	/// 1. `state.app_chid` still resolves in the app repo (not abandoned).
1331	/// 2. After `bookmark-set` has run (state.stage ∈ {PushApp,
1332	///    FinalizeClaude}), the bookmark points at a commit whose chid
1333	///    matches `state.app_chid`.
1334	/// 3. `state.claude_chid` still resolves in `.claude`.
1335	///
1336	/// On any mismatch: error with the exact `vc-x1 push <bookmark>
1337	/// --restart` remediation. A fresh state (no chids yet) is a no-op —
1338	/// nothing to verify.
1339	fn verify_state_sanity(root: &Path, state: &PushState) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
1340	    let Some(app_chid) = &state.app_chid else {
1341	        return Ok(()); // OK: fresh state, nothing to verify yet
1342	    };
1343	    let bookmark = &state.bookmark;
1344	    let root_str = root.to_string_lossy();
1345	    let cwd = Path::new(".");
1346	
1347	    // 1. app_chid still resolves.
1348	    run(
1349	        "jj",
1350	        &[
1351	            "log",
1352	            "-r",
1353	            app_chid,
1354	            "--no-graph",
1355	            "-T",
1356	            "\"x\"",
1357	            "-R",
1358	            &root_str,
1359	        ],
1360	        cwd,
1361	    )
1362	    .map_err(|_| -> Box<dyn std::error::Error> {
1363	        format!(
1364	            "state-sanity: app_chid '{app_chid}' has been abandoned or no longer resolves \
1365	             in the app repo. State is stale — run `vc-x1 push {bookmark} --restart` to clear."
1366	        )
1367	        .into()
1368	    })?;
1369	
1370	    // 2. bookmark at app_chid (after bookmark-set has run).
1371	    if matches!(state.stage, Stage::PushApp | Stage::FinalizeClaude) {
1372	        let bookmark_chid = run(
1373	            "jj",
1374	            &[
1375	                "log",
1376	                "-r",
1377	                bookmark,
1378	                "--no-graph",
1379	                "-T",
1380	                "change_id.short(12)",
1381	                "-R",
1382	                &root_str,
1383	            ],
1384	            cwd,
1385	        )?;
1386	        let bookmark_chid = bookmark_chid.trim();
1387	        if bookmark_chid != app_chid.as_str() {
1388	            return Err(format!(
1389	                "state-sanity: bookmark '{bookmark}' is at chid '{bookmark_chid}' but \
1390	                 state.app_chid is '{app_chid}'. State is stale — run \
1391	                 `vc-x1 push {bookmark} --restart` to clear."
1392	            )
1393	            .into());
1394	        }
1395	    }
1396	
1397	    // 3. claude_chid still resolves.
1398	    if let Some(claude_chid) = &state.claude_chid {
1399	        let claude = claude_path(root);
1400	        let claude_str = claude.to_string_lossy();
1401	        run(
1402	            "jj",
1403	            &[
1404	                "log",
1405	                "-r",
1406	                claude_chid,
1407	                "--no-graph",
1408	                "-T",
1409	                "\"x\"",
1410	                "-R",
1411	                &claude_str,
1412	            ],
1413	            cwd,
1414	        )
1415	        .map_err(|_| -> Box<dyn std::error::Error> {
1416	            format!(
1417	                "state-sanity: claude_chid '{claude_chid}' has been abandoned or no longer \
1418	                 resolves in .claude. State is stale — run \
1419	                 `vc-x1 push {bookmark} --restart` to clear."
1420	            )
1421	            .into()
1422	        })?;
1423	    }
1424	
1425	    Ok(())
1426	}
1427	
1428	/// Verify the world matches the saved state after all stages have run.
1429	///
1430	/// Counterpart to `verify_state_sanity` (which runs pre-stage). This
1431	/// runs after the stage loop completes and confirms the post-conditions
1432	/// are real — directly addresses the 0.37.1 false-success symptom
1433	/// (push declared "completed all stages" while WC still held
1434	/// uncommitted changes from a stale-state resume).
1435	///
1436	/// Three checks:
1437	///
1438	/// 1. App's bookmark is at a commit whose chid matches `state.app_chid`.
1439	/// 2. App's working copy has no uncommitted changes (commit-app should
1440	///    have captured anything that was there).
1441	/// 3. `.claude`'s bookmark is at `state.claude_chid`'s commit (when set).
1442	///    `.claude`'s WC may legitimately have new session writes from the
1443	///    push run itself — finalize-claude (detached) handles those — so
1444	///    no WC-clean check on `.claude`.
1445	///
1446	/// On any mismatch: returns `Err`. The caller surfaces as a loud
1447	/// warning rather than blocking — push has already crossed the remote
1448	/// boundary, work is live on origin, the warning prompts the user to
1449	/// investigate.
1450	fn verify_completion_sanity(
1451	    root: &Path,
1452	    state: &PushState,
1453	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
1454	    let bookmark = &state.bookmark;
1455	    let root_str = root.to_string_lossy();
1456	    let cwd = Path::new(".");
1457	
1458	    // 1. app bookmark at state.app_chid.
1459	    if let Some(app_chid) = &state.app_chid {
1460	        let actual = run(
1461	            "jj",
1462	            &[
1463	                "log",
1464	                "-r",
1465	                bookmark,
1466	                "--no-graph",
1467	                "-T",
1468	                "change_id.short(12)",
1469	                "-R",
1470	                &root_str,
1471	            ],
1472	            cwd,
1473	        )?;
1474	        let actual = actual.trim();
1475	        if actual != app_chid.as_str() {
1476	            return Err(format!(
1477	                "completion sanity: app bookmark '{bookmark}' is at chid '{actual}' but \
1478	                 state.app_chid is '{app_chid}'."
1479	            )
1480	            .into());
1481	        }
1482	    }
1483	
1484	    // 2. app WC clean (no uncommitted changes). Use the `empty`
1485	    // template — `jj diff --stat` always emits a "0 files changed"
1486	    // line even when clean, so plain output-emptiness check would
1487	    // false-positive.
1488	    if !jj_log_empty(root, "@")? {
1489	        let wc_diff = run("jj", &["diff", "--stat", "-r", "@", "-R", &root_str], cwd)?;
1490	        return Err(format!(
1491	            "completion sanity: app working copy has uncommitted changes (push completed \
1492	             but WC dirty?). Diff stat:\n{wc_diff}"
1493	        )
1494	        .into());
1495	    }
1496	
1497	    // 3. .claude's pinned bookmark (main) at state.claude_chid.
1498	    if let Some(claude_chid) = &state.claude_chid {
1499	        let claude = claude_path(root);
1500	        let claude_str = claude.to_string_lossy();
1501	        let actual = run(
1502	            "jj",
1503	            &[
1504	                "log",
1505	                "-r",
1506	                SESSION_BOOKMARK,
1507	                "--no-graph",
1508	                "-T",
1509	                "change_id.short(12)",
1510	                "-R",
1511	                &claude_str,
1512	            ],
1513	            cwd,
1514	        )?;
1515	        let actual = actual.trim();
1516	        if actual != claude_chid.as_str() {
1517	            return Err(format!(
1518	                "completion sanity: .claude bookmark '{SESSION_BOOKMARK}' is at chid \
1519	                 '{actual}' but state.claude_chid is '{claude_chid}'."
1520	            )
1521	            .into());
1522	        }
1523	    }
1524	
1525	    Ok(())
1526	}
1527	
1528	/// Return the 12-character change ID for `rev` in `repo`.
1529	fn get_change_id(repo: &Path, rev: &str) -> Result<String, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
1530	    let out = run(
1531	        "jj",
1532	        &[
1533	            "log",
1534	            "-r",
1535	            rev,
1536	            "--no-graph",
1537	            "-T",
1538	            "change_id.short(12)",
1539	            "-R",
1540	            &repo.to_string_lossy(),
1541	        ],
1542	        Path::new("."),
1543	    )?;
1544	    Ok(out.trim().to_string())
1545	}
1546	
1547	/// True when the given revision is empty (no working-copy changes
1548	/// relative to its parent).
1549	fn jj_log_empty(repo: &Path, rev: &str) -> Result<bool, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
1550	    let out = run(
1551	        "jj",
1552	        &[
1553	            "log",
1554	            "-r",
1555	            rev,
1556	            "--no-graph",
1557	            "-T",
1558	            "empty",
1559	            "-R",
1560	            &repo.to_string_lossy(),
1561	        ],
1562	        Path::new("."),
1563	    )?;
1564	    match out.trim() {
1565	        "true" => Ok(true),
1566	        "false" => Ok(false),
1567	        other => Err(format!("jj_log_empty: unexpected template output {other:?}").into()),
1568	    }
1569	}

aiTitle: Review bug number one from bugs.md

content.text:
Root cause found and reproduced. **This is a real vc-x1 bug, but the trigger is environmental: the detached finalize child is killed by the Claude Code sandbox before it ever runs, so the `.claude` push silently never happens.**

## The mechanism

`vc-x1 push` never pushes `.claude` itself. Its last stage shells out to `vc-x1 finalize --squash --push main --delay 10 --detach` (`src/push.rs:1276`), which spawns a `setsid`'d child (`src/finalize.rs:528`) that sleeps 10 seconds, then squashes, sets the bookmark, and pushes. The parent exits 0 immediately, so push declares "completed all stages" and clears its state file.

When the bot runs `vc-x1 push` through a sandboxed Bash command — which in tprobe is *always* (`sandbox.enabled: true`, `autoAllowBashIfSandboxed: true`, `allowUnsandboxedCommands: false`, and `vc-x1 push *` is permission-allowed), the sandbox tears down as soon as the foreground command exits and kills every process inside it. `setsid` escapes the process group but not the sandbox's pid namespace. The child dies during its 10-second sleep, before its first `jj` command — no squash, no push, no failure marker (it's SIGKILLed, and even a failing child couldn't leave one: tprobe's sandbox doesn't allow writes to `~/.cache/vc-x1`, and `write_failure_marker` deliberately swallows write errors).

## Evidence

- tprobe app-repo op log: 8 `push bookmark main to git remote origin` ops — the in-process `push-app` stage worked every cycle (network to `*.github.com` is sandbox-allowed).
- tprobe `.claude` op log: only `snapshot` / `commit` / `point bookmark` ops — **not one squash or push operation since the repo was cloned on 07-13**. The child never executed anything, ever.
- `jj bookmark list --all-remotes -R tprobe/.claude`: `main@origin (behind by 8 commits)` — matching your GitHub observation exactly.
- tprobe's `.vc-x1/` state dir is empty — every push "completed all stages" from vc-x1's perspective.
- Direct reproduction in this session: I spawned a `setsid` child from a sandboxed command that would write a file after 12s. The file was never written and the process is gone — sandboxed detached children do not survive the command's exit.

"Sometimes" pushed = whenever push runs from a real (unsandboxed) terminal the child survives — vc-x1's own `.claude` op log shows healthy squash+push pairs (last on 07-08).

## Why nothing ever surfaces it

- The child dies by SIGKILL — no marker, and marker writes are best-effort anyway (Bugs #3 territory).
- `verify_completion_sanity` only checks *local* bookmark positions, which are all correct — and the push happens 10s after push exits, so it couldn't check anyway.
- The next cycle's preflight (`verify_tracking`, `sync --check` on the app repo) never compares `.claude main` against `main@origin`, even though jj knows locally it's 8 behind.
- Side effect worth noting: the trailing-session-data squash also never happens, so that data just rides into the next cycle's commit (misplaced, not lost).

## Fix directions (the bot thinks)

1. **Stop detaching from push** — run finalize inline (delay + squash + push in-process) so the work completes inside the command's lifetime. Costs ~10s per push; removes the entire failure class for bot-driven pushes.
2. **Detect at preflight** — before starting a new cycle, error if `.claude main` is ahead of `main@origin` (jj already has the answer: "behind by 8 commits"). This is a cheap backstop regardless of option 1.
3. Immediate tprobe recovery is trivial: `jj git push --bookmark main -R ~/data/prgs/rust/tprobe/.claude` from an unsandboxed shell.

Want me to update bugs.md #1 with this diagnosis, and/or open a cycle for a fix (I'd suggest option 2 as the backstop plus option 1 or a `--no-detach` path for sandboxed use)?

message.content:
We definitely need to detect, but it shouldn't happen and we need to devise a solution.
The desire is that after pushing both repos the result is that @ entry is empty in
bot repos. We do that but executing spawning `vc-x1 finalize --delay 10 ..` so finalize
but waits 10 seconds to give "you" the bot to shutdown and remain quite. I then does
the squash and push. So that achieves the goal but is brittle as hell, as we've learned.

I think the solution is we have a mode that does both repos and we forego the goal of
an empty @. But, if the session information is "too much" we can achieve close to the goal
by having the bot execute a second `vc-x1 finalize` with squash and no delay which will
capture the first command output and thus in the session data we'll see the first
`vc-x1 ..` command.

Not sure I explained it properly but hopefully you get the idea. Thoughts?

content.text:
I get the idea, and I think it's the right direction. Let me restate it crisply and then flag one ordering pitfall and a nice property you get for free.

## The core insight you're circling

The "empty @ after push" goal is *inherently* unachievable in the exact sense: the act of finalizing is itself recorded as new session data, so there's always one command's worth of tail you can't capture — a self-referential chase. The `--delay 10 --detach` approach tried to solve it with *time-based* quiescence ("wait for the bot to go quiet"), which requires outliving the Bash command — exactly what the sandbox forbids. Your second-command idea replaces time-based quiescence with **causal ordering**, which is strictly better: by the time the bot issues command 2, the harness has already written command 1's invocation *and result* (and even command 2's own invocation) into the transcript. So an inline `finalize --squash` with **no delay at all** deterministically captures everything except its own result. "One command behind" is the natural fixed point, and your scheme reaches it without any sleeping or detaching.

## The one pitfall: ordering of squash vs. push

If the new push mode pushes `.claude` inline ("does both repos") and *then* the optional second finalize squashes the tail into that commit, the squash **rewrites an already-pushed commit** — main moves to the rewritten commit and the follow-up push becomes a force-push of published history (and jj's immutability guard fires, which is why finalize already carries the ugly `--ignore-immutable`). In the linear-journal session repo that's survivable, but it's history rewrite on a shared remote every single cycle.

The fix is to put the `.claude` push in the *second* command, not the first:

1. `vc-x1 push` — everything it does today inline (commits, bookmarks, app push), but **no detach, no delay, and no `.claude` push**. It ends by saying "run `vc-x1 finalize --squash --push main --repo .claude` to finish".
2. Bot immediately runs that finalize inline: squash tail (which now includes push's full transcript entry) into the still-unpushed session commit, advance main, push. No rewrite, no force, no `--ignore-immutable` needed on this path.

Same two commands you described, just with the remote boundary moved to command 2.

## The free property: it self-heals

If the bot forgets or fails to run command 2, nothing is lost — the session commit is bookmarked locally and *next cycle's* `jj git push --bookmark main` carries it up automatically (pushing main pushes all its ancestors). The remote lags one cycle instead of forever. And your "forego empty @" mode is just the degenerate case of this design: skip command 2 and the tail rides into the next cycle's commit.

## Detection stays regardless

The backstop belongs in push preflight: error (or warn + auto-push — it's always a safe forward move) when `.claude main` is ahead of `main@origin`. jj already computes it ("behind by 8 commits"), so it's one bookmark query. This catches every variant of this failure class, including ones we haven't invented yet.

Net: I'd retire `--detach`/`--delay` from the push flow entirely (finalize can keep the flags for standalone use, or deprecate them later), make push stop before the session push, and codify the two-command sequence in the cycle protocol. Want me to write this up as a Todo entry (with the design points above) and update Bugs #1 with the diagnosis, so it's ready to pick up as a cycle?
