330	     anchor-heavy work warranting its own cycle.
331	
332	## Bugs
333	
334	_See [bugs.md](bugs.md)._
335	
336	## Done
337	
338	Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
339	and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.
340	
341	_Migrated to [done.md](done.md) on 2026-05-15 (0.44.0–0.50.0 batch)._
342	
343	- chores subdir reshape — `notes/chores-*.md` → `notes/chores/`; 0.44.0–0.50.0 Done batch migrated to done.md (0.51.0) [[2]]
344	- `sb_ide` elimination — banner off by default (`-V` toggles), `bm_track` → `debug!`, `sb_ide` + `SubcommandRunner::{is_detached_exec, suppress_banner}` removed (0.52.0) [[3]]
345	- todo renumber + `notes/fix-todo.py` interim script; cycle re-scoped at close-out, scope CLI cleanup deferred to 0.54.0 (0.53.0) [[4]]
346	- scope CLI cleanup — `--scope` roles-only, `--por` boolean replaces `ScopeKind`, `Scope` relocated to `options_flags/`, sync gains `-R` (0.54.0) [[5]]
347	- validate-todo / fix-todo subcommands — check + renumber `## Todo` / `## Bugs` entry numbering, replacing `notes/fix-todo.py` (0.55.0) [[6]]
348	- refine cycle protocol — one protocol (Preparation/Work-N/Close-out), `.`-separator nested numbering with trailing-`0`=Preparation, push & squash discretionary, `.claude` once per push, two-gate review (work then message, both before commit), CLAUDE.md cycle/commit/push docs consolidated into one linear `## Cycle Protocol` (~39% smaller) (0.56.0) [[7]]
349	- add `--merge` todo entry — Todo #1 records future `vc-x1 push --merge` flag (close-out shape, sibling to planned `--squash`); dogfoods the Preparation/Work-N/Close-out protocol on a deliberately small docs cycle (0.57.0) [[8]]
350	- notes/todo restructure — split `## Bugs` → `bugs.md` and the long-tail `## Todo` → `todo-backlog.md`; `## Priorities` with tier sub-headings (`### P1`/`### P2`/`### P3`); CLAUDE.md `## File reads` rule + protocol codification (chores title-only during cycle, In Progress moves into chores at close-out, problem+plan shape) (0.58.0) [[9]]
351	- extract cycle protocol — `notes/cycle-protocol.md` becomes the canonical self-contained home for the cycle workflow (504 lines, extensively tightened from the CLAUDE.md extract); CLAUDE.md keeps a 10-line pointer; `notes/substep-protocol.md` folded in as `## Sub-cycle ladders`; `## Ideas` section added to `notes/todo.md`; first squash close-out via manual Option F (app squash + bot-side `af60f979` trailer rewrite + force-push) (0.59.0) [[11]]
352	- consolidate notes conventions — three notes-file sections (`Todo format`, `Reference numbering`, `Retiring Done entries`) move from notes/README.md into new CLAUDE.md `## Notes file conventions` umbrella alongside existing `## Chores conventions`; `[[N]]` citation duplicate dropped; cargo cycle (`fmt` / `clippy` / `test` / `install`) surfaced at CLAUDE.md `## Cycle Protocol` and notes/README.md (had been buried in cycle-protocol.md since 0.59.0); README.md `## Contributing` rewritten against current anchor homes (0.60.0) [[12]]
353	- por/dual parity design — eight-commit audit + design cycle producing `notes/design-cli/por-dual-parity-audit.md` as the canonical CLI-design doc (audit + commonality + feature axes + 5-layer resolution chain + subcommand × parameter matrix + per-axis Decisions blocks); new sibling `notes/design-cli/copying.md` stub for the broader file-copy mechanism that subsumes `--config` / `--gitignore` / `--use-template`; `notes/design-cli/` subdir created and three design notes regrouped under it; 14 implementation gaps seeded for 0.62.0+ cycles; one Todo promoted (`validate-desc` / `fix-desc` equalization, cheapest prototype for the topology-from-config rule) (0.61.0) [[13]]
354	- apply max review #1 — applied six concerns, four nits, and the process observation from the `max-review-1` working list to the por/dual parity design + copying stub; reframed Todo #1 (push validate body intro), seeded pre-commit-single-rule + `validate-numbering` Todos; working list fully drained, then retired (deleted — git history holds it) (0.62.0) [[14]]
355	- docs: adopt AGENTS.md — rename `CLAUDE.md` → `AGENTS.md` (Zed and the agent-tooling ecosystem default to it); one-line `@AGENTS.md` import shim at `CLAUDE.md` keeps Claude Code auto-loading; live `CLAUDE.md` references repointed to `AGENTS.md` so links resolve in editors and on GitHub; history prose (`chores-01..12` / `done.md`) left as written, with only the 3 navigational anchor links in the `chores-10/11/12` headers repointed (0.63.0) [[15]]
356	- docs: tighten after-finalize rule — rename to "After push or finalize: stop and wait" (both triggers named) and spell out that `vc-x1 push` bundles the push + `vc-x1 finalize` on `.claude` as tail stages, so all closing words go *before* invoking the wrapper and nothing is emitted after it returns (0.63.1) [[16]]
357	- docs: codify merge-non-ff recipe — promote the merge-non-ff close-out recipe to a `### Merge non-ff recipe` subsection in cycle-protocol.md (rebase → `jj new` lift → push + post-hoc caveat); reword `### Shape at close-out push` (work-done framing, Merge non-ff tagged default); standardize jj rebase `-d` → `--onto`/`-o` in AGENTS.md and drop the post-amend `jj new` note (the recipe now owns the empty-`@` why); also clarified the Preparation step (Cargo.lock, In-Progress move wording) (0.64.0) [[17]]
358	- docs: record finalize ochid-loss bug (0.65.1) — bugs.md gains the fc finalize ochid-drop incident as Bugs #1 with the fix queued as Todo #1; fc AGENTS.md additions ported (jj-not-git, one-command-per-invocation, push-injects-trailers, ochid resolvability + `.vc-config.toml`); stale chores-10 "active file" prose genericized in notes/README.md + ARCHITECTURE.md [[18]]
359	- fix: refuse ochid-dropping squash (0.65.2) — `finalize` refuses a squash that would drop source-only `ochid:` trailers (`extract_ochids` / `ochids_at_risk` / `check_squash_keeps_ochids` + tests), guarding in preflight and again in `finalize_exec` after `--delay`; failure-marker surfacing moved after the command's output with a historical banner and the `error=` value flattened; README manual-test section + `support/gen-exmpl-1-3.sh` regenerator [[19]]
360	- feat: reposition @ onto synced bookmark (0.66.0) — after a successful `--no-check` sync, `@` is repositioned onto the just-synced bookmark: code repo `jj new <b>` when clean (or `--rebase`/prompt-gated rebase when dirty; left in place when diverged/ahead), `.claude` always `jj new main` (or errors when `@-` is off main), all as a final pass *outside* the `op_restore` revert region; replaces `ensure_at_on_main`; new `--rebase` flag; README `### sync` docs + examples [[20]]
361	- feat: single-mode sync + revert command (0.67.0) — plain `vc-x1 sync` is one atomic operation (fetch, converge bookmark, reposition `@`; `--no-check` gone, `--check` a hidden deprecated alias for push preflight); failures stop for inspection with each repo's pre-sync op id persisted to `.vc-x1/sync-state.toml`; new `vc-x1 revert` restores from the snapshots; TDD via the two-clone `tests/cli_sync.rs` regression test of the t1A/t1B scenario [[21]]
362	- docs: todo cleanup + trapezoid entries (0.67.1) — push-related todos reshaped around the trapezoidal (merge non-ff) workflow: new #1 bookmark-invariant fix and #2 push pause point; "record uncovered code commits (N:1)" re-scoped to code worked outside vc-x1; `push --squash` demoted to todo-backlog.md; cycle-protocol.md push-wrapper list synced [[22]]
363	- feat: pin bot repo to main (0.68.0) — `--bookmark` is code-repo-only in push and sync; the session repo's side of every step (tracking preflight, classify/act, `bookmark-set` — renamed from `bookmark-both` — `finalize --push`, completion sanity) is pinned to `main`; plus two mid-cycle sync fixes: `reposition_session` no-ops when `@-` is the `main` tip, and the clean case prints one `nothing to sync` summary line [[23]]
364	
365	# References
366	
367	[0]: AGENTS.md#prose-form
368	[1]: /notes/design-cli/por-dual-parity.md
369	[2]: /notes/chores/chores-11.md#chore-move-chores-under-noteschores-0510

input.new_string:
1. **`vc-x1 push`: the session-repo push silently never happens
   when the bot runs push sandboxed.** Observed in
   [tprobe](https://github.com/winksaville/tprobe) 2026-07-14:
   both local repos consistent and the GitHub code repo current,
   but [tprobe.claude](https://github.com/winksaville/tprobe.claude)
   stuck 8 commits behind (`jj bookmark list --all-remotes -R
   .claude`: `main@origin (behind by 8 commits)`).
   - **Root cause (diagnosed 2026-07-14).** Push doesn't push
     `.claude` itself; its `finalize-claude` stage spawns a
     detached child that sleeps 10s first:
     - the child — `vc-x1 finalize --squash --push main
       --delay 10 --detach` — is `setsid`'d to outlive push
     - a sandboxed Bash command kills every process in its pid
       namespace at command exit; `setsid` escapes the process
       group, not the namespace
     - the child dies mid-sleep, before its first `jj` command
     - reproduced in this project's sandbox with a bare `setsid`
       child; the bot thinks the same kill explains tprobe,
       whose sandbox is always-on (`autoAllowBashIfSandboxed:
       true`, `allowUnsandboxedCommands: false`) with
       `vc-x1 push *` permission-allowed
   - **Evidence.**
     - tprobe app repo: 8 `push bookmark main` ops — the
       in-process `push-app` stage worked; network was fine
     - tprobe `.claude`: zero squash/push ops since clone — the
       child never ran even one command
     - tprobe `.vc-x1/` state dir empty — every push "completed
       all stages"
   - **Why it's silent.**
     - SIGKILL — the child never writes a failure marker
     - marker writes to `~/.cache/vc-x1` are outside the sandbox
       write allowlist anyway; `write_failure_marker` swallows
       errors by design
     - `verify_completion_sanity` checks local bookmarks only
     - related: `CliLogger::new` panics (`.expect`) when the
       `--log` path can't be opened — a sandbox denying
       `/tmp/vc-x1-finalize.log` kills the child at startup
   - **Side effect:** the trailing-data squash never runs; the
     tail rides into the next cycle's session commit (misplaced,
     not lost).
   - **Fix direction:** see the Todo "push: inline the
     session-repo push; finalize becomes the user's empty-@
     tidy-up".
   - **Recovery** for an affected project: `jj git push
     --bookmark main -R <proj>/.claude` from an unsandboxed
     shell (or any in-process command that finishes before its
     sandbox exits).

2.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Bugs

Known defects we're aware of but haven't scheduled a fix for.
Each entry describes what goes wrong, when, and the cost of
the failure. Entries are numbered (`1.` `2.` …) the same way
as `## Todo` in `todo.md`; run
`vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/bugs.md` to renumber after
insert / delete / reorder.

## Bugs

1. **vc-x1 push .. of .claude repo are sometimes not pushed**
   At https://github.com/winksaville/tprobe and the local
   The local code repo for tprobe contains:
   ```
   wink@3900x 26-07-14T18:33:27.080Z:~/data/prgs/rust/tprobe (main+1)
   $ vc-x1 list @-..
   qxsuprkxwlzy  /.claude/mxmooyzsyuuu  main | feat: no_std tprobe core
   suumzuqwnwxv  /.claude/nyptskuomvzw  chore: place pinning/perf in lazy runner crate
   ukmvsrttxmon  /.claude/ozvsoslqqrvr  chore: record iiac-perf fork intent — incidental
   zvkknwztuokt  /.claude/pqyopzzuylqo  chore: decide no_std histogram — hand-roll
   rzqzqunqtmvt  /.claude/zxovlwzryywy  docs: README Build & test, Run, Install sections
   wpnvltpmvvmm  /.claude/mkozornlsquu  feat: tp_pc version banner + -V
   llwxuznztron  /.claude/qtmskkqutmyw  feat: match tp_pc flags to iiac-perf + --decimals
   zntovlvqqsxx  /.claude/rvstvppuxuny  chore: drop ssh driver scripts for unsandboxed ssh
   xzrkvxwktmlw  /.claude/uvtmpzozkowk  feat: 3900x run-length sweep + remote protocol
   xuwynvturzws  /.claude/nxltlpvmmoqp  feat: seed no_std core + tp_pc parity example
   uynvlokxmrus  /.claude/wnwssolzozlp  feat: seed no_std core + tp_pc parity example
   lppvkqzpsqmt  /.claude/puspmmyotxww  docs: no_std core phase split + unwrap lints
   xwlvxuxyorrr  /.claude/xmrqyzymulzt  chore: scaffold crate, open no_std core cycle
   vpxqvwtnvxok  /.claude/xzzmntkqtryk  Initial commit
   wink@3900x 26-07-14T18:46:42.642Z:~/data/prgs/rust/tprobe (main+1)
   ```
   The local .claude repo matches:
   ```
   wink@3900x 26-07-14T18:46:07.908Z:~/data/prgs/rust/tprobe/.claude (main*+1)
   $ vc-x1 list @-..
   mxmooyzsyuuu  /qxsuprkxwlzy          main | feat: no_std tprobe core
   nyptskuomvzw  /suumzuqwnwxv          chore: place pinning/perf in lazy runner crate
   ozvsoslqqrvr  /ukmvsrttxmon          chore: record iiac-perf fork intent — incidental
   pqyopzzuylqo  /zvkknwztuokt          chore: decide no_std histogram — hand-roll
   zxovlwzryywy  /rzqzqunqtmvt          docs: README Build & test, Run, Install sections
   mkozornlsquu  /wpnvltpmvvmm          feat: tp_pc version banner + -V
   qtmskkqutmyw  /llwxuznztron          feat: match tp_pc flags to iiac-perf + --decimals
   rvstvppuxuny  /zntovlvqqsxx          chore: drop ssh driver scripts for unsandboxed ssh
   nxltlpvmmoqp  /xuwynvturzws          feat: seed no_std core + tp_pc parity example
   wnwssolzozlp  /uynvlokxmrus          feat: seed no_std core + tp_pc parity example
   puspmmyotxww  /lppvkqzpsqmt          docs: no_std core phase split + unwrap lints
   xmrqyzymulzt  /xwlvxuxyorrr          chore: scaffold crate, open no_std core cycle
   xzzmntkqtryk  /vpxqvwtnvxok          Initial commit
   wink@3900x 26-07-14T18:46:12.274Z:~/data/prgs/rust/tprobe/.claude (main*+1)
   ```
   And on github at https://github.com/winksaville/tprobe/commits/main/ we see
   there are only 5 commits, the last being "feat: seed no_std core + tp_pc parity example".

   I believe this is a bug in vc-x1.
  
2. **`finalize` squash silently drops the source journal's
   ochids.** `finalize_exec` (`src/finalize.rs`) always runs
   `jj squash --ignore-immutable --use-destination-message
   --from <source> --into <target>`, so when the source
   commit's message carries `ochid:` trailers the squash
   discards them with the rest of the message — the
   destination's message wins unconditionally.
   - **When it bites:** the squash source (`@`) is a
     *described* journal instead of a bare trailing-data
     snapshot. Observed sequence (fc op log, 2026-06-08):
     the bot wrote the journal message via
     `jj describe wyrlsuusnyzz` while that change was still
     the uncommitted `@`; finalize then squashed
     `--from @ --into @-` where `@-` was the previous,
     already-pushed journal — destination message won,
     journal message + 6 ochids discarded. Finalize's
     assumption (`@` is disposable, `@-` holds the real
     message) inverts whenever the journal is described on
     `@` rather than committed first.
   - **Cost:** every code-side commit pointing at the
     squashed journal is left with a dangling
     `ochid: /.claude/<chid>` — the code↔session cross-link
     breaks. Recovery requires op-log surgery on the machine
     that still has the original object (this happened in the
     fc project: journal `wyrlsuusnyzz`, 6 dangling app
     ochids, recovered 2026-06-10 from a backup workspace's
     op log plus a `.claude main` force-push).
   - **Fix direction:** before squashing, detect `ochid:`
     trailers in the source message that the destination
     lacks; refuse (or merge the trailers into the
     destination message) instead of dropping them.

3. **`finalize::surface_previous_failures` is racy and
   bounded by "next vc-x1 run".** The current model has
   four gaps:
   - **Stale forever.** Markers sit on disk until the
     next `vc-x1 <anything>` runs. If the user abandons
     the workspace (e.g., CI / scheduled use), failures
     are never surfaced.
   - **Concurrent surface_previous_failures.** Two
     `vc-x1` runs racing: both `read_dir`, both print
     the marker, one deletes (the other's `remove_file`
     silently fails). The user sees the same failure
     printed twice.
   - **Mid-write torn read.** A `finalize --exec` child
     writing a marker while a sibling `vc-x1` is
     surfacing could read partial content. Atomic-rename
     on write would close this.
   - **No notify-at-failure path.** A detached
     `finalize --exec` failure only becomes visible when
     the user next runs *any* `vc-x1` command — fine for
     interactive use, invisible for CI / scheduled use
     where there may be no next run.

   The exec-child gate in `main.rs` (since 0.52.0-3)
   patches one related case — the detached child eating
   its own prior markers before the user can see them —
   but doesn't address any of the above. Holistic fix
   needs locking + atomic writes + maybe a
   notify-at-failure path for hands-off use.

4. **`init --repo local` bare remotes keep HEAD at
   `refs/heads/master`.** The only branch pushed is `main`,
   so a later `jj git clone` of that bare repo has no default
   branch to auto-track and `vc-x1 clone` fails its
   `verify_tracking` check ("bookmark 'main' has non-tracking
   remote 'main@origin'"). Found building `tests/cli_sync.rs`
   (worked around there with `git symbolic-ref HEAD
   refs/heads/main`).
   - **Fix direction:** init's local bare provisioning sets
     HEAD to `refs/heads/main` at creation.

5. **`clone` session-remote derivation mismatches init's
   local naming; relative TARGET breaks the session clone.**
   Two related defects, both found building
   `tests/cli_sync.rs`:
   - `derive_session_url` maps `<x>.git` →
     `<x>.claude.git`, but `init --repo local` names the
     session remote `remote-claude.git` — so a dual clone of
     a locally-init'd project's `remote-code.git` looks for
     `remote-code.claude.git` and fails.
   - A relative local-path TARGET fails on the session side
     regardless: `clone_dual` runs the session `jj git
     clone` with the just-cloned code repo as cwd, so the
     relative source no longer resolves. Workaround: pass an
     absolute TARGET.

# References

input.new_string:
1. **push: inline the session-repo push; finalize becomes the
   user's empty-@ tidy-up.** Push's `finalize-claude` stage
   detaches a delayed child to squash+push `.claude`; a
   sandboxed bot run kills the child at command exit —
   silently, every cycle (see Bugs #1 [[24]]). The empty-@
   goal behind the detach/delay dance is self-referential for
   the bot (finalizing is itself session data), so the bot
   forgoes it; only the user, acting after the turn, can
   capture the full tail.
   - push runs everything inline — no detach, no delay:
     - fold the micro-tail into the session commit (squash)
     - `jj git push --bookmark main -R .claude` in-process
     - a session-push failure is a visible push failure
   - preflight backstop: `.claude main` ahead of `main@origin`
     is an error (or auto-push — always a safe forward move);
     catches this whole failure class
   - `finalize` repurposed as the user's tidy-up — zero-arg:
     squash `@ → @-` in `.claude`, push `main`, inline
     - run by the user after the bot goes quiet → both repos'
       `@` truly empty
     - the squash rewrites the just-pushed session commit, so
       its push is a forced update — acceptable as a
       deliberate user action, not an every-cycle bot one
     - safe anytime: `@` already empty → "nothing to fold",
       exit 0
     - the bot may also run it as a separate later command to
       capture the `vc-x1 push` record (leaves only its own)
   - retire `--detach` / `--delay`; decide the surviving flag
     surface (`--repo`/`--squash`/`--push` for standalone use,
     or collapse to the zero-arg form)
   - docs: cycle-protocol per-commit flow + README; the
     "After push or finalize: stop and wait" rule and the
     Recovery section rewrite around the new shape

94	3. **Version-number protocol is fragile — versions are
95	   baked into titles/bodies/todo/done/chores before the
96	   change lands.** The cycle protocol embeds an `X.Y.Z-N`
97	   version in commit titles and bodies, `## Todo` /
98	   `## Done` entries, and chores headers — all written
99	   while the work is in progress, i.e. before it lands.
100	   But version numbers are subject to change: in a public,
101	   merge-based flow (e.g. Linux), the version a change
102	   ships under is only fixed when it merges into `main`,
103	   so the landing version can't be anticipated while the
104	   work is underway. Pervasive version-in-text is

402	## feat: pin bot repo to main (0.68.0)
403	
404	`vc-x1 push <bookmark>` applied the one bookmark name to both
405	repos (preflight tracking check, `bookmark-both`,
406	`finalize --push`), and sync's classify/fetch used the passed
407	bookmark for every repo — but the bot repo is a linear journal
408	on `main` by design. Pushing a feature bookmark would create
409	and push that bookmark in the bot repo, leave the bot `main`
410	behind, and wedge the next sync's `reposition_session`. Prereq
411	for the trapezoidal-commit workflow (branch the code repo; bot
412	stays on `main`).
413	
414	Two adjacent sync warts surfaced (and were fixed) mid-cycle
415	while dogfooding: the unconditional `jj new main` on the
416	session repo, and the three-lines-to-say-nothing clean-case
417	output.
418

422	  todo, open chores section

424	  preflight + classify/act use a per-repo bookmark; tests
425	- 0.68.0-2 sync: `reposition_session` no-ops when `@-` is
426	  already the `main` tip — previously it always `jj new
427	  main`ed (empty `@`: chid/op churn; non-empty `@`: live
428	  session writes stranded on a sibling head); tests

file.content:
+ wrapper-list sync + this section.

## feat: pin bot repo to main (0.68.0)

`vc-x1 push <bookmark>` applied the one bookmark name to both
repos (preflight tracking check, `bookmark-both`,
`finalize --push`), and sync's classify/fetch used the passed
bookmark for every repo — but the bot repo is a linear journal
on `main` by design. Pushing a feature bookmark would create
and push that bookmark in the bot repo, leave the bot `main`
behind, and wedge the next sync's `reposition_session`. Prereq
for the trapezoidal-commit workflow (branch the code repo; bot
stays on `main`).

Two adjacent sync warts surfaced (and were fixed) mid-cycle
while dogfooding: the unconditional `jj new main` on the
session repo, and the three-lines-to-say-nothing clean-case
output.

### As-built ladder

- 0.68.0-0 prep: backfill Commits:, bump version, pick up
  todo, open chores section
- 0.68.0-1 sync: session repo pins `main` — tracking
  preflight + classify/act use a per-repo bookmark; tests
- 0.68.0-2 sync: `reposition_session` no-ops when `@-` is
  already the `main` tip — previously it always `jj new
  main`ed (empty `@`: chid/op churn; non-empty `@`: live
  session writes stranded on a sibling head); tests
- 0.68.0-3 sync: quiet output — clean case prints one
  summary line (`UP_TO_DATE_MSG` const shared with main.rs's
  long_about); per-repo "@ already on" no-op lines demoted
  to debug
- 0.68.0-4 push: every stage's session-repo side uses
  `main`, never the passed bookmark
  - preflight verifies the session repo tracks `main`
  - bookmark stage sets app → `<bookmark>`, session →
    `main`; renamed `bookmark-both` → `bookmark-set` (no
    legacy alias — nothing external invokes the old name)
  - finalize-claude pushes `main`
  - completion sanity checks the session repo's `main`
  - `PushState.bookmark` holds the code-repo bookmark only
  - test: a feature-bookmark push advances the session
    repo's `main` and creates no `feature` bookmark there
- 0.68.0 close-out: this section; README sync/push updates;
  wrapper planned-list sync

input.new_string:
- 0.68.0 close-out: this section; README sync/push updates;
  wrapper planned-list sync

## docs: diagnose silent session-push loss (0.68.1)

Bugs #1 reported the session repo "sometimes not pushed"
(tprobe: GitHub bot repo 8 commits behind while both local
repos and the GitHub code repo agreed). Diagnosed 2026-07-14;
this cycle records the diagnosis and queues the fix design as
Todo #1. Single-commit cycle.

- Root cause: push's `finalize-claude` stage delegates the
  session push to a detached (`setsid`) child that sleeps 10s
  first; a sandboxed Bash command kills its pid namespace at
  command exit, so a bot-run push loses the child every time —
  before its first `jj` command.
- Evidence: tprobe app repo has 8 `push bookmark main` ops;
  its `.claude` has zero squash/push ops since clone;
  reproduced with a bare `setsid` child in this project's
  sandbox.
- Silent by construction:
  - SIGKILL leaves no failure marker
  - `~/.cache/vc-x1` is outside the sandbox write allowlist
    and `write_failure_marker` swallows errors by design
  - `verify_completion_sanity` checks local state only
- Fix design (Todo #1): push does everything inline (squash
  micro-tail + `jj git push` in-process); preflight errors
  when `.claude main` is ahead of `main@origin`; `finalize`
  becomes the user's zero-arg empty-@ tidy-up, run after the
  bot goes quiet. The empty-@ goal is self-referential for
  the bot — finalizing is itself session data — so only the
  user can capture the full tail.

### As-built ladder

- 0.68.1 single-commit cycle: bugs.md diagnosis rewrite,
  Todo #1 fix design, Done entry, 0.68.0 `Commits:`
  backfill, version bump + this section.

# References

aiTitle: Review bug number one from bugs.md

content.text: Now the Done-entry migration. First, check which of the refs cited by the batch I'll move (0.51.0–0.65.2) are still cited elsewhere in todo.md:

input.command: grep -no '\[\[[0-9]*\]\]' notes/todo.md | sort -t: -k2 | uniq -c -f0 | awk '{print $1}' | sort -t'[' -k3 -n | head; echo ===; grep -no '\[\[[0-9]*\]\]' notes/todo.md
input.description: List all reference citations in todo.md with line numbers

input.old_string:
_Migrated to [done.md](done.md) on 2026-05-15 (0.44.0–0.50.0 batch)._

- chores subdir reshape — `notes/chores-*.md` → `notes/chores/`; 0.44.0–0.50.0 Done batch migrated to done.md (0.51.0) [[2]]
- `sb_ide` elimination — banner off by default (`-V` toggles), `bm_track` → `debug!`, `sb_ide` + `SubcommandRunner::{is_detached_exec, suppress_banner}` removed (0.52.0) [[3]]
- todo renumber + `notes/fix-todo.py` interim script; cycle re-scoped at close-out, scope CLI cleanup deferred to 0.54.0 (0.53.0) [[4]]
- scope CLI cleanup — `--scope` roles-only, `--por` boolean replaces `ScopeKind`, `Scope` relocated to `options_flags/`, sync gains `-R` (0.54.0) [[5]]
- validate-todo / fix-todo subcommands — check + renumber `## Todo` / `## Bugs` entry numbering, replacing `notes/fix-todo.py` (0.55.0) [[6]]
- refine cycle protocol — one protocol (Preparation/Work-N/Close-out), `.`-separator nested numbering with trailing-`0`=Preparation, push & squash discretionary, `.claude` once per push, two-gate review (work then message, both before commit), CLAUDE.md cycle/commit/push docs consolidated into one linear `## Cycle Protocol` (~39% smaller) (0.56.0) [[7]]
- add `--merge` todo entry — Todo #1 records future `vc-x1 push --merge` flag (close-out shape, sibling to planned `--squash`); dogfoods the Preparation/Work-N/Close-out protocol on a deliberately small docs cycle (0.57.0) [[8]]
- notes/todo restructure — split `## Bugs` → `bugs.md` and the long-tail `## Todo` → `todo-backlog.md`; `## Priorities` with tier sub-headings (`### P1`/`### P2`/`### P3`); CLAUDE.md `## File reads` rule + protocol codification (chores title-only during cycle, In Progress moves into chores at close-out, problem+plan shape) (0.58.0) [[9]]
- extract cycle protocol — `notes/cycle-protocol.md` becomes the canonical self-contained home for the cycle workflow (504 lines, extensively tightened from the CLAUDE.md extract); CLAUDE.md keeps a 10-line pointer; `notes/substep-protocol.md` folded in as `## Sub-cycle ladders`; `## Ideas` section added to `notes/todo.md`; first squash close-out via manual Option F (app squash + bot-side `af60f979` trailer rewrite + force-push) (0.59.0) [[11]]
- consolidate notes conventions — three notes-file sections (`Todo format`, `Reference numbering`, `Retiring Done entries`) move from notes/README.md into new CLAUDE.md `## Notes file conventions` umbrella alongside existing `## Chores conventions`; `[[N]]` citation duplicate dropped; cargo cycle (`fmt` / `clippy` / `test` / `install`) surfaced at CLAUDE.md `## Cycle Protocol` and notes/README.md (had been buried in cycle-protocol.md since 0.59.0); README.md `## Contributing` rewritten against current anchor homes (0.60.0) [[12]]
- por/dual parity design — eight-commit audit + design cycle producing `notes/design-cli/por-dual-parity-audit.md` as the canonical CLI-design doc (audit + commonality + feature axes + 5-layer resolution chain + subcommand × parameter matrix + per-axis Decisions blocks); new sibling `notes/design-cli/copying.md` stub for the broader file-copy mechanism that subsumes `--config` / `--gitignore` / `--use-template`; `notes/design-cli/` subdir created and three design notes regrouped under it; 14 implementation gaps seeded for 0.62.0+ cycles; one Todo promoted (`validate-desc` / `fix-desc` equalization, cheapest prototype for the topology-from-config rule) (0.61.0) [[13]]
- apply max review #1 — applied six concerns, four nits, and the process observation from the `max-review-1` working list to the por/dual parity design + copying stub; reframed Todo #1 (push validate body intro), seeded pre-commit-single-rule + `validate-numbering` Todos; working list fully drained, then retired (deleted — git history holds it) (0.62.0) [[14]]
- docs: adopt AGENTS.md — rename `CLAUDE.md` → `AGENTS.md` (Zed and the agent-tooling ecosystem default to it); one-line `@AGENTS.md` import shim at `CLAUDE.md` keeps Claude Code auto-loading; live `CLAUDE.md` references repointed to `AGENTS.md` so links resolve in editors and on GitHub; history prose (`chores-01..12` / `done.md`) left as written, with only the 3 navigational anchor links in the `chores-10/11/12` headers repointed (0.63.0) [[15]]
- docs: tighten after-finalize rule — rename to "After push or finalize: stop and wait" (both triggers named) and spell out that `vc-x1 push` bundles the push + `vc-x1 finalize` on `.claude` as tail stages, so all closing words go *before* invoking the wrapper and nothing is emitted after it returns (0.63.1) [[16]]
- docs: codify merge-non-ff recipe — promote the merge-non-ff close-out recipe to a `### Merge non-ff recipe` subsection in cycle-protocol.md (rebase → `jj new` lift → push + post-hoc caveat); reword `### Shape at close-out push` (work-done framing, Merge non-ff tagged default); standardize jj rebase `-d` → `--onto`/`-o` in AGENTS.md and drop the post-amend `jj new` note (the recipe now owns the empty-`@` why); also clarified the Preparation step (Cargo.lock, In-Progress move wording) (0.64.0) [[17]]
- docs: record finalize ochid-loss bug (0.65.1) — bugs.md gains the fc finalize ochid-drop incident as Bugs #1 with the fix queued as Todo #1; fc AGENTS.md additions ported (jj-not-git, one-command-per-invocation, push-injects-trailers, ochid resolvability + `.vc-config.toml`); stale chores-10 "active file" prose genericized in notes/README.md + ARCHITECTURE.md [[18]]
- fix: refuse ochid-dropping squash (0.65.2) — `finalize` refuses a squash that would drop source-only `ochid:` trailers (`extract_ochids` / `ochids_at_risk` / `check_squash_keeps_ochids` + tests), guarding in preflight and again in `finalize_exec` after `--delay`; failure-marker surfacing moved after the command's output with a historical banner and the `error=` value flattened; README manual-test section + `support/gen-exmpl-1-3.sh` regenerator [[19]]
- feat: reposition @ onto synced bookmark (0.66.0)

-- docs: codify merge-non-ff recipe — promote the merge-non-ff close-out recipe to a `### Merge non-ff recipe` subsection in cycle-protocol.md (rebase → `jj new` lift → push + post-hoc caveat); reword `### Shape at close-out push` (work-done framing, Merge non-ff tagged default); standardize jj rebase `-d` → `--onto`/`-o` in AGENTS.md and drop the post-amend `jj new` note (the recipe now owns the empty-`@` why); also clarified the Preparation step (Cargo.lock, In-Progress move wording) (0.64.0) [[17]]

-- docs: record finalize ochid-loss bug (0.65.1) — bugs.md gains the fc finalize ochid-drop incident as Bugs #1 with the fix queued as Todo #1; fc AGENTS.md additions ported (jj-not-git, one-command-per-invocation, push-injects-trailers, ochid resolvability + `.vc-config.toml`); stale chores-10 "active file" prose genericized in notes/README.md + ARCHITECTURE.md [[18]]

-- fix: refuse ochid-dropping squash (0.65.2) — `finalize` refuses a squash that would drop source-only `ochid:` trailers (`extract_ochids` / `ochids_at_risk` / `check_squash_keeps_ochids` + tests), guarding in preflight and again in `finalize_exec` after `--delay`; failure-marker surfacing moved after the command's output with a historical banner and the `error=` value flattened; README manual-test section + `support/gen-exmpl-1-3.sh` regenerator [[19]]

- feat: reposition @ onto synced bookmark (0.66.0) — after a successful `--no-check` sync, `@` is repositioned onto the just-synced bookmark: code repo `jj new <b>` when clean (or `--rebase`/prompt-gated rebase when dirty; left in place when diverged/ahead), `.claude` always `jj new main` (or errors when `@-` is off main), all as a final pass *outside* the `op_restore` revert region; replaces `ensure_at_on_main`; new `--rebase` flag; README `### sync` docs + examples [[20]]

- feat: single-mode sync + revert command (0.67.0) — plain `vc-x1 sync` is one atomic operation (fetch, converge bookmark, reposition `@`; `--no-check` gone, `--check` a hidden deprecated alias for push preflight); failures stop for inspection with each repo's pre-sync op id persisted to `.vc-x1/sync-state.toml`; new `vc-x1 revert` restores from the snapshots; TDD via the two-clone `tests/cli_sync.rs` regression test of the t1A/t1B scenario [[21]]

- docs: todo cleanup + trapezoid entries (0.67.1) — push-related todos reshaped around the trapezoidal (merge non-ff) workflow: new #1 bookmark-invariant fix and #2 push pause point; "record uncovered code commits (N:1)" re-scoped to code worked outside vc-x1; `push --squash` demoted to todo-backlog.md; cycle-protocol.md push-wrapper list synced [[22]]

- todo renumber + `notes/fix-todo.py` interim script; cycle re-scoped at close-out, scope CLI cleanup deferred to 0.54.0 (0.53.0) [[98]]

- validate-todo / fix-todo subcommands — check + renumber `## Todo` / `## Bugs` entry numbering, replacing `notes/fix-todo.py` (0.55.0) [[100]]
- refine cycle protocol — one protocol (Preparation/Work-N/Close-out), `.`-separator nested numbering with trailing-`0`=Preparation, push & squash discretionary, `.claude` once per push, two-gate review (work then message, both before commit), CLAUDE.md cycle/commit/push docs consolidated into one linear `## Cycle Protocol` (~39% smaller) (0.56.0) [[101]]
- add `--merge` todo entry — Todo #1 records future `vc-x1 push --merge` flag (close-out shape, sibling to planned `--squash`); dogfoods the Preparation/Work-N/Close-out protocol on a deliberately small docs cycle (0.57.0) [[102]]
- notes/todo restructure — split `## Bugs` → `bugs.md` and the long-tail `## Todo` → `todo-backlog.md`; `## Priorities` with tier sub-headings (`### P1`/`### P2`/`### P3`); CLAUDE.md `## File reads` rule + protocol codification (chores title-only during cycle, In Progress moves into chores at close-out, problem+plan shape) (0.58.0) [[103]]

- consolidate notes conventions — three notes-file sections (`Todo format`, `Reference numbering`, `Retiring Done entries`) move from notes/README.md into new CLAUDE.md `## Notes file conventions` umbrella alongside existing `## Chores conventions`; `[[N]]` citation duplicate dropped; cargo cycle (`fmt` / `clippy` / `test` / `install`) surfaced at CLAUDE.md `## Cycle Protocol` and notes/README.md (had been buried in cycle-protocol.md since 0.59.0); README.md `## Contributing` rewritten against current anchor homes (0.60.0) [[105]]

- apply max review #1 — applied six concerns, four nits, and the process observation from the `max-review-1` working list to the por/dual parity design + copying stub; reframed Todo #1 (push validate body intro), seeded pre-commit-single-rule + `validate-numbering` Todos; working list fully drained, then retired (deleted — git history holds it) (0.62.0) [[107]]
- docs: adopt AGENTS.md — rename `CLAUDE.md` → `AGENTS.md` (Zed and the agent-tooling ecosystem default to it); one-line `@AGENTS.md` import shim at `CLAUDE.md` keeps Claude Code auto-loading; live `CLAUDE.md` references repointed to `AGENTS.md` so links resolve in editors and on GitHub; history prose (`chores-01..12` / `done.md`) left as written, with only the 3 navigational anchor links in the `chores-10/11/12` headers repointed (0.63.0) [[108]]
- docs: tighten after-finalize rule — rename to "After push or finalize: stop and wait" (both triggers named) and spell out that `vc-x1 push` bundles the push + `vc-x1 finalize` on `.claude` as tail stages, so all closing words go *before* invoking the wrapper and nothing is emitted after it returns (0.63.1) [[109]]
- docs: codify merge-non-ff recipe — promote the merge-non-ff close-out recipe to a `### Merge non-ff recipe` subsection in cycle-protocol.md (rebase → `jj new` lift → push + post-hoc caveat); reword `### Shape at close-out push` (work-done framing, Merge non-ff tagged default); standardize jj rebase `-d` → `--onto`/`-o` in AGENTS.md and drop the post-amend `jj new` note (the recipe now owns the empty-`@` why); also clarified the Preparation step (Cargo.lock, In-Progress move wording) (0.64.0) [[110]]
- docs: record finalize ochid-loss bug (0.65.1) — bugs.md gains the fc finalize ochid-drop incident as Bugs #1 with the fix queued as Todo #1; fc AGENTS.md additions ported (jj-not-git, one-command-per-invocation, push-injects-trailers, ochid resolvability + `.vc-config.toml`); stale chores-10 "active file" prose genericized in notes/README.md + ARCHITECTURE.md [[111]]
- fix: refuse ochid-dropping squash (0.65.2) — `finalize` refuses a squash that would drop source-only `ochid:` trailers (`extract_ochids` / `ochids_at_risk` / `check_squash_keeps_ochids` + tests), guarding in preflight and again in `finalize_exec` after `--delay`; failure-marker surfacing moved after the command's output with a historical banner and the `error=` value flattened; README manual-test section + `support/gen-exmpl-1-3.sh` regenerator [[112]]

+- consolidate notes conventions — three notes-file sections (`Todo format`, `Reference numbering`, `Retiring Done entries`) move from notes/README.md into new CLAUDE.md `## Notes file conventions` umbrella alongside existing `## Chores conventions`; `[[N]]` citation duplicate dropped; cargo cycle (`fmt` / `clippy` / `test` / `install`) surfaced at CLAUDE.md `## Cycle Protocol` and notes/README.md (had been buried in cycle-protocol.md since 0.59.0); README.md `## Contributing` rewritten against current anchor homes (0.60.0) [[105]]

+- por/dual parity design — eight-commit audit + design cycle producing `notes/design-cli/por-dual-parity-audit.md` as the canonical CLI-design doc (audit + commonality + feature axes + 5-layer resolution chain + subcommand × parameter matrix + per-axis Decisions blocks); new sibling `notes/design-cli/copying.md` stub for the broader file-copy mechanism that subsumes `--config` / `--gitignore` / `--use-template`; `notes/design-cli/` subdir created and three design notes regrouped under it; 14 implementation gaps seeded for 0.62.0+ cycles; one Todo promoted (`validate-desc` / `fix-desc` equalization, cheapest prototype for the topology-from-config rule) (0.61.0) [[106]]

+- apply max review #1 — applied six concerns, four nits, and the process observation from the `max-review-1` working list to the por/dual parity design + copying stub; reframed Todo #1 (push validate body intro), seeded pre-commit-single-rule + `validate-numbering` Todos; working list fully drained, then retired (deleted — git history holds it) (0.62.0) [[107]]

+- docs: adopt AGENTS.md — rename `CLAUDE.md` → `AGENTS.md` (Zed and the agent-tooling ecosystem default to it); one-line `@AGENTS.md` import shim at `CLAUDE.md` keeps Claude Code auto-loading; live `CLAUDE.md` references repointed to `AGENTS.md` so links resolve in editors and on GitHub; history prose (`chores-01..12` / `done.md`) left as written, with only the 3 navigational anchor links in the `chores-10/11/12` headers repointed (0.63.0) [[108]]

+- docs: tighten after-finalize rule — rename to "After push or finalize: stop and wait" (both triggers named) and spell out that `vc-x1 push` bundles the push + `vc-x1 finalize` on `.claude` as tail stages, so all closing words go *before* invoking the wrapper and nothing is emitted after it returns (0.63.1) [[109]]

+- docs: codify merge-non-ff recipe — promote the merge-non-ff close-out recipe to a `### Merge non-ff recipe` subsection in cycle-protocol.md (rebase → `jj new` lift → push + post-hoc caveat); reword `### Shape at close-out push` (work-done framing, Merge non-ff tagged default); standardize jj rebase `-d` → `--onto`/`-o` in AGENTS.md and drop the post-amend `jj new` note (the recipe now owns the empty-`@` why); also clarified the Preparation step (Cargo.lock, In-Progress move wording) (0.64.0) [[110]]

+- docs: record finalize ochid-loss bug (0.65.1) — bugs.md gains the fc finalize ochid-drop incident as Bugs #1 with the fix queued as Todo #1; fc AGENTS.md additions ported (jj-not-git, one-command-per-invocation, push-injects-trailers, ochid resolvability + `.vc-config.toml`); stale chores-10 "active file" prose genericized in notes/README.md + ARCHITECTURE.md [[111]]

+- fix: refuse ochid-dropping squash (0.65.2) — `finalize` refuses a squash that would drop source-only `ochid:` trailers (`extract_ochids` / `ochids_at_risk` / `check_squash_keeps_ochids` + tests), guarding in preflight and again in `finalize_exec` after `--delay`; failure-marker surfacing moved after the command's output with a historical banner and the `error=` value flattened; README manual-test section + `support/gen-exmpl-1-3.sh` regenerator [[112]]

content.text:
Right — that's the plan, but it isn't implemented yet. This cycle (0.68.1) was docs-only: it recorded the diagnosis in bugs.md and queued the fix design as Todo #1. The push code itself is unchanged, so `vc-x1 push` still runs the detached finalize — it succeeded this time only because the push ran unsandboxed (which is also why the sync tests passed preflight).

The actual code change — push doing the session squash+push inline, the preflight `main`-vs-`main@origin` backstop, `finalize` becoming the zero-arg user tidy-up, and retiring `--detach`/`--delay` from the push path — is the next cycle's work, picking up Todo #1 (presumably 0.69.0).

Want me to open that cycle now (prep commit: backfill 0.68.1's `Commits:`, bump to 0.69.0-0, move Todo #1 into In Progress with a plan ladder)?

input.old_string: _No cycle currently in progress._
input.new_string:
**feat: inline session push + user finalize (0.69.0)**

Push's `finalize-claude` stage detaches a delayed child to
squash+push `.claude`; a sandboxed bot run kills the child at
command exit — silently, every cycle (see Bugs #1 [[24]]). The
empty-@ goal behind the detach/delay dance is self-referential
for the bot (finalizing is itself session data), so the bot
forgoes it; only the user, acting after the turn, can capture
the full tail.

Design notes:
- the repurposed `finalize` squash rewrites the just-pushed
  session commit, so its push is a forced update — acceptable
  as a deliberate user action, not an every-cycle bot one
- safe anytime: `@` already empty → "nothing to fold", exit 0
- the bot may also run `finalize` as a separate later command
  to capture the `vc-x1 push` record (leaves only its own)
- flag surface open: keep `--repo`/`--squash`/`--push` for
  standalone use, or collapse to the zero-arg form

Plan:
- 0.69.0-0 prep: backfill Commits:, bump version, pick up
  Todo #1, open chores section (done)
- 0.69.0-1 push: replace the finalize-claude shell-out with
  an in-process squash of the micro-tail + `jj git push
  --bookmark main -R .claude`; a session-push failure is a
  visible push failure; tests
- 0.69.0-2 push preflight backstop: error (or auto-push)
  when `.claude main` is ahead of `main@origin`; tests
- 0.69.0-3 finalize: zero-arg user tidy-up — squash `@ → @-`
  in `.claude` + push `main`, inline, no delay; retire
  `--detach`/`--delay`; decide surviving flag surface; tests
- 0.69.0-4 docs: cycle-protocol rewrite ("After push or
  finalize: stop and wait" rule, Recovery section), README
- 0.69.0 close-out and validation

forgoes it; only the user, acting after the turn, can capture
the full tail.

Design notes:
- the repurposed `finalize` squash rewrites the just-pushed
  session commit, so its push is a forced update — acceptable
  as a deliberate user action, not an every-cycle bot one
- safe anytime: `@` already empty → "nothing to fold", exit 0
- the bot may also run `finalize` as a separate later command
  to capture the `vc-x1 push` record (leaves only its own)
- flag surface open: keep `--repo`/`--squash`/`--push` for
  standalone use, or collapse to the zero-arg form

- 0.69.0 close-out and validation

   goal behind the detach/delay dance is self-referential for

   capture the full tail.

     - a session-push failure is a visible push failure
   - preflight backstop: `.claude main` ahead of `main@origin`

     catches this whole failure class

2	
3	This file contains near term tasks with a short description

6	Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every

10	## In Progress

16	   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
17	   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah

39

41	- 0.69.0-0 prep: backfill Commits:, bump version, pick up
42	  Todo #1, open chores section (done)
43	- 0.69.0-1 push: replace the finalize-claude shell-out with

47	- 0.69.0-2 push preflight backstop: error (or auto-push)
48	  when `.claude main` is ahead of `main@origin`; tests
49	- 0.69.0-3 finalize: zero-arg user tidy-up — squash `@ → @-`

52	- 0.69.0-4 docs: cycle-protocol rewrite ("After push or
53	  finalize: stop and wait" rule, Recovery section), README
54	- 0.69.0 close-out and validation
55	
56	## Todo
57	
58	 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
59	 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
60	 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/todo.md` to renumber.
61	 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
62	 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
63	 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
64	 number. Long-tail entries
65	 live in [todo-backlog.md](todo-backlog.md). Use the
66	 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](/AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
67	 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
68	 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).
69	
70	1. **vc-x1 push: pause point between commit and publish
71	   stages.** The merge non-ff close-out is a three-step
72	   sequence:
73	   - commit the close-out pair locally (normal 1:1 commit
74	     stages, ochids injected both directions)
75	   - rebase the code-side close-out into the merge (chids
76	     survive rebase, so every ochid stays valid)
77	   - publish
78	
79	   Push has no supported stop after `commit-claude`, so today
80	   the recipe pre-commits both sides manually and resumes via
81	   `--from bookmark-set --yes` — skipping exactly the stages
82	   that inject `ochid:` trailers.
83	   - Add a stop after the commit stages (`--to commit-claude`
84	     or `--no-publish`; name open); the existing `--from
85	     bookmark-set` is already the resume half.
86	   - Retires the close-out workaround; the merge commit
87	     carries its code→bot ochid because it was injected
88	     normally, before the rebase.
89	   - Together with the Todo "push/sync: bookmark is
90	     code-repo-only; pin the bot repo to main", completes
91	     the trapezoidal-commit workflow (1:1 bot↔code
92	     throughout; the merge is a code-side-only shape
93	     operation).
94	
95	2. **Version-number protocol is fragile — versions are
96	   baked into titles/bodies/todo/done/chores before the
97	   change lands.** The cycle protocol embeds an `X.Y.Z-N`
98	   version in commit titles and bodies, `## Todo` /
99	   `## Done` entries, and chores headers — all written
100	   while the work is in progress, i.e. before it lands.
101	   But version numbers are subject to change: in a public,
102	   merge-based flow (e.g. Linux), the version a change
103	   ships under is only fixed when it merges into `main`,
104	   so the landing version can't be anticipated while the
105	   work is underway. Pervasive version-in-text is
106	   therefore fragile for any non-linear / multi-contributor
107	   workflow. Promoted from Ideas at 0.65.2-0; slated for
108	   the cycle after 0.65.2.
109	   - Open question: what identifies a cycle's commits if
110	     not a pre-assigned version?
111	     - Needs to be unique within some agreed upon domain.
112	       A contributors email address would do it, but also
113	       a UUID (short-version) for a contribution. I could
114	       imagine a UUID generated from the initial email/issue
115	       that and then "version number" schema appended to that.
116	   - Surfaces to update once the identifier is chosen:
117	     cycle-protocol.md (title shape, Numbering), AGENTS.md
118	     (commit-recording headers), and the `vc-x1` validators
119	     that parse `(X.Y.Z)` strings.
120	3. **sync follow-up: extract `move-bookmark` command.** The
121	   "put the bookmark / `@` where it belongs" step at the end
122	   of sync (reposition logic) is useful standalone — e.g. the
123	   t1B scenario where `main` is right but `@` isn't on it —
124	   and deserves an honestly-named command instead of a mode.
125	   - `vc-x1 move-bookmark` (name open): no fetch; move `@`
126	     (and optionally the bookmark) onto a target under the
127	     same safety rules as sync's reposition step.
128	   - Sync's final step becomes a call to the same logic.
129	   - Follow-up to the 0.67.0 single-mode sync cycle.
130	4. **sync follow-up: push preflight in-process; drop
131	   `--check`; revisit push auto-rollback.** Push's preflight
132	   shells out to `vc-x1 sync --check` — a verify-only pass
133	   that is both racy (remote can move before the user's
134	   later apply) and not actually read-only (jj's fetch
135	   auto-ffs tracked bookmarks). Follow-up to the 0.67.0
136	   single-mode sync cycle.
137	   - Preflight becomes a real in-process sync (stop-on-error
138	     halts the push before anything is committed); the
139	     `sync --check` shell-out and PATH dependency go away.
140	   - Remove the deprecated hidden `--check` alias once
141	     nothing invokes it.
142	   - Apply the stop-on-error + `vc-x1 revert` philosophy to
143	     push's commit-stage rollback (today it auto-runs
144	     `jj op restore`, hiding the evidence).
145	5. **validate-numbering: rename the pair, check all
146	   sequence-managed notes files generically.** `validate-todo`
147	   / `fix-todo` only operate on the single file passed, so a
148	   renumber slip in `bugs.md`, `todo-backlog.md`, or
149	   `todo.md`'s `## Ideas` section passes unnoticed — too weak
150	   for a pre-commit gate. Prereq for the pre-commit doc
151	   validators (Todo "pre-commit: single rule ...").
152	   - Rename the pair: `validate-todo` → `validate-numbering`,
153	     `fix-todo` → `fix-numbering` — they validate numbered-
154	     sequence integrity, not todos specifically.
155	   - Generic detection: for every `#…#` section, validate the
156	     column-0 `^\d+\.␠` entries form a contiguous 1..N run.
157	     Drops the Todo/Bugs special-casing; auto-covers
158	     `## Ideas` and any new numbered section. Keep the
159	     column-0 anchor so indented sub-lists aren't counted.
160	   - Default scope: a fixed list of sequence-managed notes

All good — the session repo did push. On 3900x (the authoritative machine), `.claude` shows `main`, `main@git`, and `main@origin` all at `chore: prep inline-session-push cycle (0.69.0-0)` — the detached finalize squashed the tail and pushed successfully, so GitHub's `vc-x1.claude` is current through 0.69.0-0 (and the 0.68.1 commit before it).

The second shell in your paste is `nps-gnc@rpi5-20cd`, not 3900x — that clone's `main` is just stale at 0.68.0 because it hasn't fetched since; its empty `@` is also expected, since no bot session writes happen on that machine. A `jj git fetch -R .claude` (or `vc-x1 sync`) on the rpi5 should bring it up to 0.69.0-0.

Ready to start 0.69.0-1 (inline session squash+push in `stage_finalize_claude`'s place) whenever you say go.

1	//! Shared platform handle passed to every subcommand operation.
2	//!
3	//! `Context` holds platform state that is the same across every
4	//! subcommand: today, the loaded `UserConfig` and the resolved
5	//! `--log` path. Built once at CLI startup and threaded through
6	//! to the subcommand layer.
7	//!
8	//! The shape is deliberately minimal — fields like project root,
9	//! progress sinks, etc. are added when a real consumer surfaces.
10	//! See `ARCHITECTURE.md` for the CLI-args vs subcommand
11	//! Context+Params layering rationale.
12	
13	use std::path::PathBuf;
14	
15	use crate::config::{self, UserConfig};
16	
17	/// Shared platform handle for subcommand operations.
18	///
19	/// - `user_config`: the loaded user config
20	///   (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml` or its discovered equivalent).
21	/// - `log`: the `--log` path, if given — where file-bound output
22	///   goes (and what a re-exec'd child is told to log to).
23	pub struct Context {
24	    pub user_config: UserConfig,
25	    pub log: Option<PathBuf>,
26	}
27	
28	impl Context {
29	    /// Build a `Context` by loading the user config from disk and
30	    /// taking the CLI-resolved `--log` path.
31	    pub fn load(log: Option<PathBuf>) -> Result<Self, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
32	        Ok(Self {
33	            user_config: config::load()?,
34	            log,
35	        })
36	    }
37	}
38

1	//! Integration tests for the push module.
2	//!
3	//! End-to-end tests for `push_in` against real dual-repo jj
4	//! fixtures (bare-git remotes + colocated jj repos under a
5	//! unique tempdir via `crate::test_helpers::Fixture`).
6	//!
7	//! Every test uses `--from message` to skip `preflight` (no
8	//! `Cargo.toml` in the fixture) and `--no-finalize` to avoid
9	//! spawning a detached `vc-x1 finalize` child that would
10	//! outlive the test. The remaining stages (message,
11	//! commit-app, commit-claude, bookmark-set, push-app) are
12	//! exercised against the fixture's local bare-git remote.
13	//!
14	//! Stage execution + rollback are covered here;
15	//! state-file / layout / stage-ordering mechanics are covered
16	//! in the neighboring `tests` module via pure unit tests.
17	//!
18	//! Requires `jj` and the compiled `vc-x1` binary in `PATH`.
19	
20	use super::*;
21	use crate::test_helpers::Fixture;
22	use std::fs;
23	use std::process::Command;
24	
25	/// Run `jj <args> -R <repo>` and return trimmed stdout on success.
26	fn jj(repo: &Path, args: &[&str]) -> String {
27	    let out = Command::new("jj")
28	        .args(args)
29	        .arg("-R")
30	        .arg(repo)
31	        .output()
32	        .expect("spawn jj");
33	    assert!(
34	        out.status.success(),
35	        "jj {args:?} failed in {}: {}",
36	        repo.display(),
37	        String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stderr)
38	    );
39	    String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stdout).trim().to_string()
40	}
41	
42	/// Commit ID (short, 12 chars) for a revision.
43	fn cid(repo: &Path, rev: &str) -> String {
44	    jj(
45	        repo,
46	        &["log", "-r", rev, "--no-graph", "-T", "commit_id.short(12)"],
47	    )
48	}
49	
50	/// Full description of a revision.
51	fn description(repo: &Path, rev: &str) -> String {
52	    jj(repo, &["log", "-r", rev, "--no-graph", "-T", "description"])
53	}
54	
55	/// First line of a revision's description.
56	fn desc_first_line(repo: &Path, rev: &str) -> String {
57	    jj(
58	        repo,
59	        &[
60	            "log",
61	            "-r",
62	            rev,
63	            "--no-graph",
64	            "-T",
65	            "description.first_line()",
66	        ],
67	    )
68	}
69	
70	/// Standard test params: bookmark=main, `--from message` (skip
71	/// preflight), `--no-finalize` (skip detached finalize),
72	/// `--yes` (auto-approve any interactive prompts).
73	fn test_params(title: &str, body: &str) -> PushParams {
74	    PushParams {
75	        bookmark: Some("main".to_string()),
76	        restart: false,
77	        from: Some(Stage::Message),
78	        step: false,
79	        status: false,
80	        recheck: false,
81	        no_finalize: true,
82	        dry_run: false,
83	        title: Some(title.to_string()),
84	        body: Some(body.to_string()),
85	        yes: true,
86	    }
87	}
88	
89	/// Happy path when `.claude` has no pending changes: the app
90	/// commit lands with an `ochid` trailer pointing at `.claude`'s
91	/// pre-existing `@-`, `commit-claude` is skipped, and both
92	/// `bookmark-set` + `push-app` still run cleanly.
93	#[test]
94	fn push_happy_claude_clean() {
95	    let fx = Fixture::new("push-clean");
96	    fs::write(fx.work.join("hello.txt"), "hi").expect("write app file");
97	
98	    let claude_main_before = cid(&fx.claude, "main");
99	
100	    push_in(&fx.work, &test_params("feat: clean case", "app body")).expect("push should succeed");
101	
102	    // App repo: main advanced to our new commit.
103	    assert_eq!(desc_first_line(&fx.work, "main"), "feat: clean case");
104	    let app_full = description(&fx.work, "main");
105	    assert!(
106	        app_full.contains("ochid: /.claude/"),
107	        "app ochid trailer missing:\n{app_full}"
108	    );
109	
110	    // `.claude` main unchanged (no commit happened there).
111	    assert_eq!(
112	        cid(&fx.claude, "main"),
113	        claude_main_before,
114	        ".claude main should not have moved"
115	    );
116	}
117	
118	/// Happy path when `.claude` has pending changes: both repos
119	/// commit, each with an ochid trailer pointing at the other.
120	#[test]
121	fn push_happy_claude_dirty() {
122	    let fx = Fixture::new("push-dirty");
123	    fs::write(fx.work.join("app.txt"), "app").expect("write app file");
124	    fs::write(fx.claude.join("session.jsonl"), "{\"line\":1}\n").expect("write session file");
125	
126	    let claude_main_before = cid(&fx.claude, "main");
127	
128	    push_in(&fx.work, &test_params("feat: paired change", "paired body"))
129	        .expect("push should succeed");
130	
131	    // Both repos have new commits with matching titles.
132	    assert_eq!(desc_first_line(&fx.work, "main"), "feat: paired change");
133	    assert_eq!(desc_first_line(&fx.claude, "main"), "feat: paired change");
134	
135	    // Cross-repo ochid trailers are both present.
136	    let app_full = description(&fx.work, "main");
137	    let claude_full = description(&fx.claude, "main");
138	    assert!(
139	        app_full.contains("ochid: /.claude/"),
140	        "app ochid missing:\n{app_full}"
141	    );
142	    // `.claude`'s ochid points at the app repo, so the prefix is
143	    // just `/` (no `.claude` segment).
144	    assert!(
145	        claude_full
146	            .lines()
147	            .any(|l| l.starts_with("ochid: /") && !l.starts_with("ochid: /.claude/")),
148	        ".claude ochid should point at app repo:\n{claude_full}"
149	    );
150	
151	    // `.claude` main moved off its initial commit.
152	    assert_ne!(
153	        cid(&fx.claude, "main"),
154	        claude_main_before,
155	        ".claude main should have advanced"
156	    );
157	}
158

160	/// app repo grows + pushes `feature`, while `.claude` advances and
161	/// keeps only `main` — no `feature` bookmark may appear there.
162	#[test]
163	fn push_feature_bookmark_pins_session_to_main() {
164	    let fx = Fixture::new("push-feature-pin");
165	    fs::write(fx.work.join("app.txt"), "app").expect("write app file");

content.text:
Work complete. Please review.

Summary of the 0.69.0-1 diff:

- **src/push.rs** — `stage_finalize_claude` no longer shells out to `vc-x1 finalize --delay 10 --detach`. It now builds `FinalizeParams` (squash `@ → @-`, push `main`, delay 0, no detach) and calls the new `finalize::finalize_inline` in-process, so the squash and session push complete before push returns and any failure fails the push visibly. This also drops the `vc-x1`-on-PATH dependency and the `/tmp/vc-x1-finalize.log` special case. Doc comments updated (`Stage::FinalizeClaude`, the stage fn, `verify_completion_sanity`'s note about the `.claude` tail).
- **src/finalize.rs** — new `finalize_inline(params)`: preflight + `finalize_exec`, no `Context`, no detach branch. The existing ochid-drop guard (Bugs #2) runs on this path unchanged. The `finalize` subcommand itself is untouched (its `--detach`/`--delay` retirement is step -3).
- **src/push/integration_tests.rs** — two new tests, both passing against real fixtures with bare origins:
  - `push_finalize_claude_inline_pushes_session`: a full push (without `--no-finalize`) lands `.claude main` on origin synchronously and leaves `@` empty.
  - `push_finalize_claude_folds_micro_tail`: a session write landing after `commit-claude` gets squashed into the session commit (change id preserved, so app-side ochids stay valid) and pushed.
- **notes/todo.md** — ladder -1 marked (done).

`cargo fmt` / `clippy -D warnings` / `cargo test` (392 passed) all clean; 0.69.0-0→-1 binary installed. One behavioral note for your review: with the delay gone, push itself no longer waits for *any* post-command session writes — the tail now rides to the next push or gets captured by your manual `finalize`, exactly per the Todo #1 design (and step -3 makes that finalize zero-arg).

Both issues are fixed:

- **Installed binary restored**: `~/.cargo/bin/vc-x1` is now built from the pushed 0.69.0-0 commit tree (exported to scratchpad, so provenance is exact) — the other instance sees only prep-cycle code, which is identical to 0.68.1 behavior. My earlier -1 install is fully unwound; sorry about that — the missed version bump is also why it was invisible to `-V`.
- **Per-commit versioning corrected**: working copy is now `0.69.0-1` in Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock, matching the every-commit-bumps convention from the 0.68.0 cycle. From here on `-V` distinguishes each work commit.

On the rename question: I'd recommend **against** changing `package.name` — it renames the binary and ripples through everything that expects `vc-x1` (the fixture setup and push preflight shell out to `vc-x1` on PATH, README, completions). Two lighter options for the rest of this cycle while your other instance runs:

1. **Skip the install step per work commit** (deviation from protocol, restored at close-out or once the other instance is done) — simplest, and preflight/tests still exercise the new code from the repo.
2. **Dogfood under a side name**: `cargo build --release && cp target/release/vc-x1 ~/.cargo/bin/vc-x1-dev` — no manifest changes, PATH `vc-x1` stays stable, and we can trial `vc-x1-dev push` for real when we want to see the inline finalize work end-to-end.

I'd do (2) — it keeps the dogfood signal without touching the shared binary. Which would you like? (Meanwhile the -1 diff itself is unchanged and still ready for your review.)

aiTitle: Review bug number one from bugs.md

content.content:
1	//! Shared harness for CLI subprocess integration tests.
2	//!
3	//! Lives under `tests/common/` so Cargo treats it as a shared module
4	//! (a top-level `tests/common.rs` would be compiled as its own test
5	//! crate). Each `tests/<name>.rs` declares `mod common;` to opt in.
6	//!
7	//! Cargo compiles each `tests/*.rs` as its own crate; helpers used
8	//! by some test crates but not others get dead-code warnings in the
9	//! crates that don't reach them. The crate-level allow below mutes
10	//! that — it's the standard idiom for shared `tests/common/`.
11	//!
12	//! - `vc_x1()` returns a `Command` for the binary Cargo built for
13	//!   the current test crate and is the value returned by
14	//!   `env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_vc-x1")`.
15	//! - `run_ok` / `run_err` wrap `Command::output` with a panic on
16	//!   the unexpected exit status, embedding stdout/stderr in the
17	//!   message so failures are debuggable.
18	//! - `CliFixture` owns a per-test tempdir under `$TMPDIR` and
19	//!   removes it on drop. Subprocesses get `HOME` overridden to a
20	//!   tempdir-scoped path so user config can't leak into or out of
21	//!   the test.
22	
23	#![allow(dead_code)]
24	
25	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
26	use std::process::{Command, Output};
27	use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU64, Ordering};
28	use std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};
29	
30	// Single source of truth for tempdir-root resolution, shared with
31	// the binary's unit-test harness via `#[path]` include. See
32	// `src/test_tmp_root.rs` for the rationale and dependency-free
33	// constraint.
34	#[path = "../../src/test_tmp_root.rs"]
35	mod test_tmp_root;
36	use test_tmp_root::{resolve_tmp_root, should_keep_tempdir};
37	
38	static COUNTER: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);
39	
40	/// Build a unique tempdir path for a CLI test.
41	///
42	/// Mirrors `src/test_helpers::unique_base`'s shape (timestamp +
43	/// per-process counter) but uses a `vc-x1-cli-test-` prefix to
44	/// distinguish from the in-process fixtures' `vc-x1-test-` paths.
45	pub fn unique_base(tag: &str) -> PathBuf {
46	    let ts = SystemTime::now()
47	        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
48	        .map(|d| d.as_nanos())
49	        .unwrap_or(0);
50	    let n = COUNTER.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst);
51	    resolve_tmp_root().join(format!("vc-x1-cli-test-{tag}-{ts}-{n}"))
52	}
53	
54	/// Build a `Command` invoking the `vc-x1` binary that Cargo built
55	/// for this test crate. Cargo sets `CARGO_BIN_EXE_vc-x1` to the
56	/// full path of the freshly-built binary; this `Command` runs it.
57	pub fn vc_x1() -> Command {
58	    Command::new(env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_vc-x1"))
59	}
60	
61	/// Run `cmd`, panicking with stdout/stderr if it does not exit 0.
62	pub fn run_ok(cmd: &mut Command) -> Output {
63	    let out = cmd.output().expect("spawn vc-x1");
64	    if !out.status.success() {
65	        panic!(
66	            "vc-x1 unexpectedly failed: status={:?}\n--- stdout ---\n{}\n--- stderr ---\n{}",
67	            out.status,
68	            String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stdout),
69	            String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stderr),
70	        );

aiTitle: Review bug number one from bugs.md

content.text:
Good catch — let me untangle the concept first, because there are actually **two tails**, and seeing that makes the naming fall out.

## What "micro-tail" actually is

The session repo is a live journal: writes arrive continuously, so *any* commit is a snapshot cut, and whatever lands after the cut is a **tail**. Our flow has two of them:

- **The micro-tail** — writes landing in the fraction of a second between `commit-claude` and the finalize stage, inside one push run. Mostly snapshot noise. This is what push's inline squash folds; it's what my test exercises at line 188.
- **The turn tail** — the big one: the push command's own transcript entry, its result, the bot's closing words, end-of-turn writes. It lands *after* push exits and is only capturable by a later command — your post-quiescence tidy-up, or it rides into the next cycle's commit.

Same phenomenon, same operation (squash `@ → @-`, push), different cut points. So I'd argue we don't need "micro-tail" as a first-class term at all — the domain concept is **the tail**, qualified by when ("the tail that accumulated during the push" vs. "the turn's tail").

## Is it bot-repo-only?

Yes, by construction. A tail exists only where a writer outlives command boundaries — the session harness. The app repo has no background writer, and push's completion sanity even *asserts* its working copy is clean. So the term belongs to the session-repo vocabulary, and its definition should live where that vocabulary lives.

## Naming the command

Given the command's essence is "capture the session tail into the journal and push it," my recommendation: **rename `finalize` → `flush`**.

- It's the exact, familiar metaphor: buffered writes → durable store. "After the bot goes quiet, run `vc-x1 flush`" reads perfectly in the protocol.
- One verb then covers both sites: push's last stage *is* an inline flush (stage name `flush-claude` or `flush-session`), and your manual run is the final flush that leaves `@` empty.
- "finalize" was named for the old end-of-step detach/delay ceremony we're deleting — the baggage goes with it.
- `micro-tail-push` I'd avoid: it names the mechanism not the intent, and it would be *wrong* for your manual run, which captures the turn tail, not the micro one.

Runner-up: `capture-tail` (most literal, clunkier to type). Cheapest option: keep `finalize` and just define "tail" properly — but I agree with your instinct that now is the moment, while -3 is already rewriting that command's surface.

## If you pick `flush`

I'd reword -1's new doc comments to "tail" vocabulary before we commit it (small edits, no behavior change), put the canonical definition in the module docstring (`flush.rs` née `finalize.rs`) plus a prose definition in cycle-protocol.md at -4, and re-scope -3 to "rename `finalize` → `flush`, zero-arg user flush, retire `--detach`/`--delay`" — with or without a deprecated `finalize` alias for one cycle, your call.

Which name do you want — `flush`, `capture-tail`, keep `finalize`, or something else?

Maybe to be more specific it could be named squash-push or sqsh-push to shorten it a little more.

I.e. a flush is in simple terms a squash + push and it is frequently needed for the bot repo, but the app repo could use it at times. On the naming, maybe to be more specific it could be named squash-push or sqsh-push to shorten it a little more. That name is more precise, thoughts?

content.text:
`squash-push` is the right call, and I'd take it exactly as you framed it: the command is mechanism-named and repo-generic (it already takes `--repo` today), while "the tail" stays as the *narrative* for why the bot repo needs it constantly. Two reactions on the specifics:

**Yes to `squash-push`, no to `sqsh-push`.** It matches jj's own vocabulary (`jj squash` + `jj git push` — a reader can guess the implementation from the name), and hyphenated verb compounds are already house style (`fix-todo`, `validate-desc`, `test-fixture`). `sqsh` saves two characters at the cost of being a decoder-ring abbreviation — the same reason AGENTS.md bans `// SE`-style comments — and tab completion makes the length moot anyway (`vc-x1 sq<tab>`).

**One honest caveat for the app-repo use case.** Squash-push onto an already-pushed tip is by definition a published-history rewrite plus forced update (it's why the squash carries `--ignore-immutable`). That's exactly what you want for "amend a typo into the commit I just pushed without the bot" — but the docs should say it plainly, so nobody mistakes it for a plain commit+push. Fine as a deliberate user action; the bot only ever invokes it on `.claude` inside push's stage.

Proposed fallout, so naming lands once and cleanly:

- **-1 (now, before commit):** reword my new doc comments to drop "micro-tail" as a term — plain "the trailing session writes that landed since `commit-claude`" — so -1 ships no vocabulary we're about to replace. Behavior untouched.
- **-3 (re-scoped):** `finalize` → `squash-push`: zero-ceremony default (`--repo .`, squash `@ → @-`, push the repo's pinned/named bookmark — for `.claude` callers that's `main`), retire `--detach`/`--delay`, rename push's stage `finalize-claude` → `squash-push-claude` and `--no-finalize` → `--no-squash-push` (state-file stage names change; an in-flight resume across the rename errors cleanly with the `--restart` hint). Decide there whether `finalize` survives one cycle as a hidden deprecated alias.
- **-4:** cycle-protocol/README define the tail once (session-repo concept) and present `squash-push` for both audiences: the user's post-quiescence flush of the turn tail, and the generic amend-and-push tool.

Want me to apply the -1 rewording now so it's ready to commit?

Design notes:
- the repurposed `finalize` squash rewrites the just-pushed
  session commit, so its push is a forced update — acceptable
  as a deliberate user action, not an every-cycle bot one
- safe anytime: `@` already empty → "nothing to fold", exit 0
- the bot may also run `finalize` as a separate later command
  to capture the `vc-x1 push` record (leaves only its own)
- flag surface open: keep `--repo`/`--squash`/`--push` for

Design notes:
- naming (decided 2026-07-15): `finalize` → `squash-push` —
  mechanism-named and repo-generic (a squash `@ → @-` +
  push). Needed frequently on the bot repo (the session
  tail), occasionally on the app repo (amend-and-push — a
  deliberate published-history rewrite + forced update).
- the squash-push of a just-pushed session commit rewrites
  it, so its push is a forced update — acceptable as a
  deliberate user action, not an every-cycle bot one
- CLI shape: `-R`/`--repo`, default `.` (house convention:
  `-R` repo, `-r` revision); positional BOOKMARK defaulting
  to `main` (mirrors push); repo stays a flag — no command
  takes a positional repo
- no-op feedback: `@` empty and bookmark already at remote →
  "repo is already sync'd with remote", exit 0; `@` empty
  but remote behind → skip the squash, still push
- the bot may also run `squash-push` as a separate later
  command to capture the `vc-x1 push` record (leaves only
  its own)

content.text:
All set. To summarize where the naming landed (now recorded in the In Progress design notes, dated):

- **`squash-push`** it is — mechanism-named, repo-generic: squash `@ → @-` + push. Frequent on the bot repo (the session tail), occasional on the app repo (amend-and-push, a deliberate rewrite + forced update).
- **CLI shape**: `-R`/`--repo` defaulting to `.` (capital `-R` per house convention — `-r` stays revision), positional BOOKMARK defaulting to `main`, repo never positional.
- **No-op semantics**: `@` empty *and* bookmark at remote → "repo is already sync'd with remote", exit 0; `@` empty but remote behind → skip the squash, still push.
- **"micro-tail" is gone** as a term — zero occurrences left; the code comments now say "trailing session writes" / "tail", and the canonical prose definition lands in cycle-protocol at -4.
- The rename itself (command, push stage `squash-push-claude`, `--no-squash-push` flag, possible deprecated `finalize` alias) is the re-scoped -3.

The -1 diff is unchanged in behavior and back to awaiting your work review: inline finalize-claude stage, `finalize_inline`, self-invocation via `current_exe`, the two new integration tests, the `vc-x1-dev` crate rename (with rename-proof test macros), version 0.69.0-1. `fmt`/`clippy`/tests all clean, and `vc-x1-dev 0.69.0-1` is installed alongside the untouched `vc-x1 0.69.0-0`.

message.content: It now says "would not be acceptable as an automatic bot step in every cycle". Actually there is nothing wrong with you doing it, and it functions fine, the problem is we won't end up with an empty @ if you use on the bot repo. But it will work as expected on any other repo.

content.text: Ah, right — the real distinction is futility, not safety. The bot running it works mechanically; it just can't ever *achieve the goal* on its own repo, because the invocation itself writes new session data — the self-reference again. On any repo without a live writer, anyone gets the expected result. Rewriting the bullet to say that:

message.content: Do we want to name "finalize_inline"
Maybe a better name for finalize.rs and finalize_inline since we're retiring finalize commadn?

content.text: Yes — and the tidy part is that `finalize_inline` doesn't even need a new name: it dissolves. Once -3 retires `--detach`/`--delay`, the `finalize()` entry point has exactly one path left — preflight + exec — which *is* `finalize_inline`. So the shim merges into the renamed command's single entry point, and push's stage calls that directly. The renames that do carry over to -3: `finalize.rs` → `squash_push.rs`, `FinalizeArgs`/`FinalizeParams` → `SquashPushArgs`/`SquashPushParams`. There's also the detach-only failure-marker machinery (`write_failure_marker` / `surface_previous_failures`) whose reason to exist retires with `--detach` — worth a deliberate decision there, since keeping the *surfacing* one more cycle would drain any stale markers left by older installed versions. Recording all of that in -3:

    `squash-push-claude`; `--no-finalize` →
    `--no-squash-push`
  - decide deprecated `finalize` alias
  - tests

message.content: WC is synonym for @?

content.text:
Effectively yes, in jj terms: "WC" is shorthand for the **working copy**, and jj materializes the working copy *as a commit* named `@`. So "the WC has uncommitted changes" and "`@` is non-empty" say the same thing — the only nuance is that "working copy" names the files on disk while `@` names the commit jj continuously snapshots them into.

Your stumbling on it is a fair signal though — it's exactly the decoder-ring abbreviation AGENTS.md tells me to avoid. Let me check where it appears:

message.content: Design notes has some long bullets use sub-bullets where appropriate

input.old_string:
Design notes:
- naming (decided 2026-07-15): `finalize` → `squash-push` —
  mechanism-named and repo-generic (a squash `@ → @-` +
  push). Needed frequently on the bot repo (the session
  tail), occasionally on the app repo (amend-and-push — a
  deliberate published-history rewrite + forced update).
- the squash-push of a just-pushed session commit rewrites
  it, so its push is a forced update. That functions fine
  whether the bot or the user runs it. But on the bot repo
  only the user gets an empty `@` out of it: the bot's own
  invocation writes new session data, so its `@` is
  non-empty again immediately. On any repo without a live
  writer it works as expected for anyone.
- CLI shape: `-R`/`--repo`, default `.` (house convention:
  `-R` repo, `-r` revision); positional BOOKMARK defaulting
  to `main` (mirrors push); repo stays a flag — no command
  takes a positional repo
- no-op feedback: `@` empty and bookmark already at remote →
  "repo is already sync'd with remote", exit 0; `@` empty
  but remote behind → skip the squash, still push
- the bot may also run `squash-push` as a separate later
  command to capture the `vc-x1 push` record (leaves only
  its own)
input.new_string:
Design notes:
- naming (decided 2026-07-15): `finalize` → `squash-push`
  - mechanism-named and repo-generic: a squash `@ → @-` +
    push
  - needed frequently on the bot repo (the session tail)
  - occasionally useful on the app repo: amend-and-push —
    a deliberate published-history rewrite + forced update
- squash-push of a just-pushed session commit rewrites it,
  so its push is a forced update
  - functions fine whether the bot or the user runs it
  - but on the bot repo only the user gets an empty `@`:
    the bot's own invocation writes new session data, so
    its `@` is non-empty again immediately
  - on any repo without a live writer it works as expected
    for anyone
- CLI shape
  - `-R`/`--repo`, default `.` (house convention: `-R`
    repo, `-r` revision)
  - positional BOOKMARK defaulting to `main` (mirrors push)
  - repo stays a flag — no command takes a positional repo

- naming (decided 2026-07-15): `finalize` → `squash-push` —
